---
  title: "Semi-targeted Metabolomics Analysis using tsatsiki_v05"
author:
  - name: Dimitrios Prymidis
affiliation:
  - Cologne Universiy
date: 2025-08-20
output: 
  html_document:
  self_contained: true
toc: true
toc_float: true
toc_depth: 5
code_folding: show
vignette: >
  %\VignetteIndexEntry{Frezza lab Metabolomics Analysis Pipeline R script}
%\VignetteEngine{knitr::rmarkdown}
bibliography: bibliography.bib
editor_options: 
  chunk_output_type: console
---
  
```{r chunk_setup, include = FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(
  collapse = TRUE,
  comment = "#>"
)
```


### Instructions!

# Press Alt+o, and then alt+shift+o , this should minimize your code. Press on the <-> to open specific parts. Now open 0. Settings.

### --- 0. Settings
# Folders
In order to run the pipeline, the folder "tsatsiki_vXX" has to be inside the ProjectFolder. The ProjectFolder has to have the name set as 'Year_name_ProjectCode'.

To Load the data you have to create a folder called "Inputs" in the project folder. In that folder there must be 3 and only files.
1. The SSF, which must be an excel file with the 'SSF' on the file name and it has to have exactly the same structure and the AG.Frezza metabolomics SSF.
2. The data must be an excel file without the 'SSF' in its name. The data must follow the Tracefinder output format (samples in rows and metabolites in columns). 
3. The sample run order in the mass spec. This must be the only csv in the folder. It is found in the Raw data folder. The data should be a table with the names of the samples ordered in the first column.

you will need to add input in
1- 0.2. Set General Parameters: To set up the project folder and folders in general, Thresholds and identifiers.
2- 0.4. Load Data:  This is to fix the sample names in the data to match the SSF and also the names in the runOrder.

Run each chunk by pressing the the Run Current Chunk on the far right side of the code ------------------------> down here!
  
#0.1. Load libraries.
```{r Load libraries}
library(tidyverse)
library(ggfortify)
library(patchwork)
library(reshape2) # for melting
library(ggrepel)  # For smart label positioning
library(gridExtra) # to combine plots
library(ggstatsplot) # for boxplots
library(pheatmap) # Heatmap
library(EnhancedVolcano) # Volcano 
library(MetaboAnalystR) # Ora
library(pathview) # for pathway visualisations
library(openxlsx) # for TF_errors, excel coloring
```

#0.2. Set Parameters.
```{r}
# Instructions

# In order to run the pipeline, the folder "tsatsiki_vXX" has to be inside the ProjectFolder. The ProjectFolder has to have the name set as 'Year_name_ProjectCode'.
# Find the project folder in the windows explorer, copy the file path and run the next line. Then, print and save the path in the variable ProjectFolder. )


### ------------- Set working directory here ----------- ###

# convert_clip <- function() {gsub("\\\\", "/", readClipboard())}
# ProjectFolder <- convert_clip()
# ProjectFolder
ProjectFolder <- "C:/Users/Dimitrios/Desktop/2025_Sebastian_CoRe"

# Get project Code
ProjectCode <- strsplit(ProjectFolder, "_")[[1]][[ length(strsplit(ProjectFolder, "_")[[1]])]]

# Set working directory
setwd(ProjectFolder) 

# Set input folder
input.dir <-  file.path(ProjectFolder, "Inputs")

# Set Output folders
output.dir <-  file.path(ProjectFolder,paste(ProjectCode,"Outputs", sep = " "))

# CoRe Analysis
CoRe = TRUE

### ---------- Set Custom Thresholds ---------- ###

#Metabolite S to N ratio
SignaltoNoiseRatio_threshold = 3

# Feature NA filtering
Feature_Filt_Value <- 0.8 # in 10 samples we allow for 2 NAs 
Feature_Filt_Value_Pools <- 0.8 # If we have 4 pools, we allow 1 NA in 4 which means 75 not 80

# RSd / CV filtering
Threshold_cv = 30

#Filter outlier values - sd threshold ?

# Set Blank sample rownames identifier. This is used to identify the blank samples from the rownames in the data.
blank_samp_id <- "BLK|Blank|blank"

# Set up valine-d8 idenrtifier
valine_pattern_id = "aline-d8|valine-D8"

paste0("#0.2. Set General Parameters Finished!")
```

#0.3. Set universe.
```{r}
Input_name_vec <- c("1,2,3-Propanetricarboxylic acid","1,5-Anhydrosorbitol","1-Methyl nicotinamide","1-Methylhistidine","1-Methylxanthine","2',3' cyclic CMP","2-Aminobenzoic acid","2-Deoxyribose","2-Hydroxyadipic acid","2-Hydroxybutyric acid","2-Hydroxyglutaric acid","2-Hydroxypyridine","2-Oxoadipic acid","2-Oxoglutaric acid","2-Phosphoglyceric acid","3-(3-Hydroxyphenyl)-3-hydroxypropanoic acid","3',5' cyclic AMP","3',5' cyclic GMP","3-Dehydroxycarnitine","3'-Dephospho-CoA","3-Guanidinopropanoic acid","3-Hydroxy-adipic acid","3-Hydroxybutyric acid","3-Hydroxyhippuric acid","3-Hydroxyisobutyric acid","3-Hydroxymethylglutaric acid","3-Methyl pyruvic acid","3-Methylhistamine","3-Methylhistidine","4,5-Dihydroorotic acid","4a-Carbinolamine tetrahydrobiopterin","4-Hydroxyphenyllactic acid","4-Hydroxyphenylpyruvic acid","5-Amino-levulinic acid","5-Hydroxyindoleacetic acid","5-Methylcytosine","5-Phosphoribosylamine","7-Methylguanine","Acetyl-CoA","Adenine","Adenosine","Adenylsuccinic acid","ADP","ADP-glucose","ADP-ribose","AICAR","AIR","Ala-Gln","Ala-Glu","Alanine","Ala-Thr","Ala-Tyr","Allantoin","Alliin","Aminoadipic acid","AMP","Anserine","Arginine","Argininosuccinic acid","Asn-Lys","Asparagine","Aspartic acid","ATP","beta-Alanine","Betaine","Bilirubin","Biliverdin","Biotin","CAR 2:0","CAR 3:0","CAR 3:0;2Me","CAR 4:0;3Me","CAR 4:0;3OH","CAR 4:1;2Me","CAR 5:0","CAR DC3:0","CAR DC4:0","CAR DC5:0","CAR DC6:0","Carbamoylaspartate","Carnitine","Carnosine","CDP","CDP-choline","CDP-Ethanolamine","Choline","Choline phosphate","Citric acid","Citrulline","CMP","CMP-N-acetylneuraminic acid","Coenzyme A","Creatine","Creatinine","CTP","Cyclic ADP-ribose","cyclic CMP","Cys-Gly","Cystathionine","Cysteic acid","Cysteine-S-sulfate","Cysteinylglycine disulfide","Cystine","Cytidine","Cytosine","dAMP","dCMP","Deoxyadenosine","Deoxycytidine","Deoxyguanosine","Deoxyinosine","Deoxyribose 5-phosphate","Deoxyuridine","dGMP","Diethanolamine","Dihydrofolic acid","Dihydrothymine","Dihydrouracil","Dihydroxyacetone phosphate","Dimethylarginine","Dimethylglycine","Dopamine","dTDP","dTDP-glucose","dTMP","dTTP") 
Input_name_vec <- c(Input_name_vec, "dUMP","dUTP","Ectoine","Epsilon-(gamma-Glutamyl)-lysine","Ergothioneine","Erythronic acid","FAD","FAICAR","FGAM","FGAR","FMN","Folic acid","Formiminoglutamic acid","Fructose 6-phosphate","Fumaric acid","Galacturonic acid","gamma-Glutamylcysteine","gamma-Glutamylglutamic acid","gamma-Glutamylglutamine","gamma-Glutamylhistidine","gamma-Glutamylvaline","GAR","GDP","GDP-L-fucose","Glucaric acid","Glucuronic acid","Glutamic acid","Glutamine","Glutarylglycine","Glutathione oxidized","Glutathione","Gly-Asp","Glyceraldehyde 3-phosphate","Glyceric acid","Glycine","Gly-Ile","Gly-Pro","Gly-Tyr","GMP","GTP","Guanidoacetic acid","Guanine","Guanosine","Heme","Hexose 6-phosphate","Histamine","Histidine","His-Val","Homoarginine","Homocitrulline","Homocysteine","Homoserine","Hydroxykynurenine","4-Hydroxyproline","Hypotaurine","Hypoxanthine","Imidazoleacetic acid","IMP","Indoleacetic acid","Indoxyl sulfate","Inosine","Inositol","Isoleucine","Itaconic acid","Ketoisovaleric acid","Ketoleucine","Kynurenic acid","Kynurenine","Lactic acid","Leucine","Lysine","Lys-Val","Maleic acid","Malic acid","Melatonin","Methionine","Methionine sulfone","Methionine sulfoxide","Methylguanidine","Methylimidazoleacetic acid","Methylmalonic acid","Methylthioadenosine","N(2)-Acetyllysine","N(6)-Methyllysine","N1,N12-Diacetylspermine","N1,N8-Diacetylspermidine","N1-Acetylspermine","N6-Acetyllysine","N-6-Trimethyllysine","N8-Acetylspermidine","N-Acetylalanine","N-Acetylasparagine","N-Acetylaspartic acid","N-Acetyl-Asp-Glu","N-Acetylglutamic acid","N-Acetylglutamine","N-Acetylhistidine","N-Acetylneuraminic acid","N-Acetylornithine","N-Acetylserotonin","N-Acetylthreonine","N-Acetyltryptophan","NAD","NADH","NADP","NADPH","N-Carbamoylaspartic acid","N-Formylglycine","N-Formylphenylalanine","Niacinamide","Nicotinamide ribotide","Nicotinic acid","N-Methylserotonin","N-Methyltryptamine","O-Acetylserine","Ophthalmic acid","Ornithine","Orotic acid","Orotidine","o-Succinylhomoserine","Oxoproline","Pantothenic acid","Phenylalanine","Phosphocreatine","Phosphoenolpyruvic acid","Phosphoserine","Picolinic acid","Pipecolic acid","Porphobilinogen","Proline","Proline betaine","Protoporphyrinogen IX","Pseudouridine","Pterin","Purine","Pyridoxal","Pyridoxine","Pyroglutamic acid","Pyruvic acid","Quinic acid","Quinolinic acid","Riboflavin","Ribonic acid","Ribose 5-phosphate","Ribothymidine","Saccharopine","S-Adenosylhomocysteine","S-Adenosylmethionine","SAICAR","Sarcosine","Sedoheptulose 7-phosphate","Ser-Ala","Serine","Serotonin","sn-Glycero-3-phosphate","Sorbitol","Spermidine","Spermine","S-Succinylglutathione","Succinic acid","Succinyl-CoA","Targinine","Taurine","Thiamine","Threonic acid","Threonine","Thymidine","Thymine","trans-Aconitic acid","Trigonelline","Trimethylamine N-oxide","Tryptamine","Tryptophan","Tyrosine","UDP","UDP-glucose","UDP-glucuronic acid","UDP-N-acetylglucosamine","UMP","Uracil","Urea","Ureidopropionic acid","Uric acid","Uridine","Urocanic acid","UTP","Val-Asp","Val-Glu","Valine","Vitamin C","Xanthine","Xanthosine","Xanthurenic acid","XMP")


Super_class_vec <- c("Organic acids","Carbohydrates","Alkaloids","Organic acids","Nucleic acids","Nucleic acids","Benzenoids","Carbohydrates","Fatty Acyls","Fatty Acyls","Fatty Acyls","Alkaloids","Fatty Acyls","Organic acids","Organic acids","Organic acids","Nucleic acids","Nucleic acids","Fatty Acyls","Fatty Acyls","Organic nitrogen compounds","Fatty Acyls","Fatty Main_class_vec <- c("Carboxylic acids","Monosaccharides","Pyridine alkaloids","Amino acids and peptides","Purines","Pyrimidines","Benzenes","Monosaccharides","Fatty acids","Fatty acids","Fatty acids","Pyridine alkaloids","Fatty acids","TCA acids","Short-chain acids","Phenylpropanoids","Purines","Purines","Fatty esters","Fatty esters","Guanidines","Fatty acids","Fatty acids","Benzamides","Fatty acids","Fatty acids","Fatty acids","Amines","Amino acids and peptides","Amino acids and peptides","Pterins","Phenylpropanoids","Phenylpropanoids","Fatty acids","Tryptophan alkaloids","Pyrimidines","Monosaccharides","Purines","Fatty esters","Purines","Purines","Purines","Purines","Purines","Purines","Imidazoles","Imidazoles","Amino acids and peptides","Amino acids and peptides","Amino acids and peptides","Amino acids and peptides","Amino acids and peptides","Cyclic ureas","Amino acids and peptides","Amino acids and peptides","Purines","Amino acids and acids and peptides","Amino acids and peptides","Amino acids and peptides","Sulfinic acids","Purines","Histidine alkaloids","Purines","Tryptophan alkaloids","Tryptophan alkaloids","Purines","Alcohols Sub_class_vec <- c("Tricarboxylic acids","Hexoses","Nicotinic acid alkaloids","Amino acids","Xanthines","Pyrimidine rNMP","Aminobenzoic acids","Pentoses","Dicarboxylic acids","Hydroxy FA","Dicarboxylic acids","Nicotinic acid alkaloids","Dicarboxylic acids","TCA acids","Short-chain acids","Cinnamic acids","Purine rNMP","Purine rNMP","Acyl carnitines","Acyl acids","Purine rNMP","Dipeptides","Amino acids","Amino acids","Dipeptides","Amino acids","Amino acids","Purine rNTP","Amino acids","Amino acids","Bilirubins","Bilirubins","Biotins","Acyl purines","Amino acids","Imidazolines","Pyrimidine rNTP","Purine ribonucleosides","Pyrimidine rNMP","Dipeptides","Amino acids","Amino acids","Amino acids","Dipeptides","Amino acids","Pyrimidine acids","Phenylethylamines","Pyrimidine dNDP","Pyrimidine ribonucleosides","Pyrimidine dNMP","Pyrimidine dNTP","Pyrimidine dNMP","Pyrimidine dNTP","Hydropyrimidines","Dipeptides","Amino acids","Sugar acids","Sugar acids","Dipeptides","Dipeptides","Dipeptides","Dipeptides","Dipeptides") 
Sub_class_vec <- c(Sub_class_vec,"Glycinamide ribonucleotides","Purine rNDP","Purine rNDP","Sugar acids","Sugar acids","Amino acids","Amino acids","Amino acids","Tripeptides","Tripeptides","Dipeptides","Short-chain acids","Sugar acids","Amino acids","Dipeptides","Dipeptides","Dipeptides","Purine rNMP","Purine rNTP","Amino acids","Hypoxanthines","Purine acids","Amino acids","Guanidines","Imidazole alkaloids","Dicarboxylic acids","Purine ribonucleosides","Amino acids","Amino acids","Polyamines","Polyamines","Polyamines","Amino acids","Amino acids","Polyamines","Amino acids","Amino acids","Amino acids","Dipeptides","Amino acids","Amino acids","Amino acids","Sugar acids","Amino acids","Tryptamines","Amino acids","Amino acids","Nicotinamide dinucleotides","Nicotinamide dinucleotides","Nicotinamide dinucleotides","Nicotinamide dinucleotides","Amino acids","Amino acids","Amino acids","Nicotinic acid alkaloids","Nicotinic acid alkaloids","Nicotinic acid alkaloids","Tryptamines","Tryptamines","Amino acids","Tripeptides","Amino acids","Pyrimidine carboxylic acids","Pyrimidine ribonucleosides","Amino acids","Amino acids","Amino acids","Amino acids","Amino acids","Short-chain acids","Amino acids","Nicotinic acid alkaloids","Amino acids","Aralkylamines","Amino acids","Amino acids","Porphyrins","Pyrimidine ribonucleosides","Pterins","Other purines","Nicotinic acid alkaloids","Nicotinic acid alkaloids","Amino acids","Short-chain acids","Hydroxybenzoic acids","Nicotinic acid alkaloids","Flavins","Sugar acids","Pentose phosphates","Pyrimidine ribonucleosides","Amino acids","Other purines","Other purines","Imidazole ribonucleosides and ribonucleotides","Amino acids","Monosaccharide phosphates","Dipeptides","Amino acids","Tryptamines","Organic phosphoric acids","Sugar alcohols","Polyamines","Polyamines","Amino acids","TCA acids","Acyl CoAs","Amino acids","Sulfonic acids","Thiamines","Sugar acids","Amino acids","Pyrimidine deoxyribonucleosides","Other pyrimidines","Tricarboxylic acids","Nicotinic acid alkaloids","Trisubstituted amine oxides","Tryptamines","Amino acids","Amino acids","Pyrimidine rNDP","Pyrimidine rNDP","Pyrimidine rNDP","Pyrimidine rNDP","Pyrimidine rNMP","Other pyrimidines","Ureas","Ureas","Xanthines","Pyrimidine ribonucleosides","Imidazole alkaloids","Pyrimidine rNTP","Dipeptides","Dipeptides","Amino acids","Gamma butyrolactones","Xanthines","Purine ribonucleosides","Acridone alkaloids","Purine rNMP" )

HMDB_ID_vec <- c("HMDB0031193","HMDB0002712","HMDB0000699","HMDB0000001","HMDB0010738","HMDB0011691","HMDB0001123","HMDB0003224","HMDB0000321","HMDB0000008","HMDB0059655","HMDB0013751","HMDB0000225","HMDB0000208","HMDB0003391","HMDB0002643","HMDB0000058","HMDB0001314","HMDB0001161","HMDB0001373","HMDB0013222","HMDB0000345","HMDB0000357","HMDB0006116","HMDB0000023","HMDB0000355","HMDB0000005","HMDB0001861","HMDB0000479","HMDB0000528","HMDB0002215","HMDB0000755","HMDB0000707","HMDB0001149","HMDB0000763","HMDB0002894","HMDB0001128","HMDB0000897","HMDB0001206","HMDB0000034","HMDB0000050","HMDB0000536","HMDB0001341","HMDB0006557","HMDB0001178","HMDB0062179","HMDB0001235","HMDB0028685","HMDB0028686","HMDB0000161","HMDB0028697","HMDB0028699","HMDB0000462","HMDB0301759","NA","HMDB0000045","HMDB0000194","HMDB0000517","HMDB0000052","HMDB0028736","HMDB0000168","HMDB0000191","HMDB0000538","HMDB0000056","HMDB0000043","HMDB0000054","HMDB0001008","HMDB0000030","HMDB0240771","HMDB0062514","HMDB0000736","HMDB0000688","HMDB0013127","HMDB0002366","HMDB0013128","HMDB0002095","HMDB0061717","HMDB0013130","HMDB0061677","HMDB0000828","HMDB0000062","HMDB0000033","HMDB0001546","HMDB0001413","HMDB0001564","HMDB0000097","HMDB0001565","HMDB0000094","HMDB0000904","HMDB0000095","HMDB0001176","HMDB0001423","HMDB0000064","HMDB0000562","HMDB0000082","HMDB0249529","NA","HMDB0000078","HMDB0000099","HMDB0303991","HMDB0000731","HMDB0000709","HMDB0000192","HMDB0000089","HMDB0000630","HMDB0000905","HMDB0001202","HMDB0000101","HMDB0000014","HMDB0000085","HMDB0000071","HMDB0001031","HMDB0000012","HMDB0001044","HMDB0004437","HMDB0001056","HMDB0304210","HMDB0000076","HMDB0001473","HMDB0001539","HMDB0000092","HMDB0000073","HMDB0001274","HMDB0001328","HMDB0001227","HMDB0001342","HMDB0001409","HMDB0001191","HMDB0240650","HMDB0003869","HMDB0003045","HMDB0000613","HMDB0001248","HMDB0001439","HMDB0002305","HMDB0001308","HMDB0001520","HMDB0000121","HMDB0000854","HMDB0003971","HMDB0000134","HMDB0002545","HMDB0001049","HMDB0011737","HMDB0011738","HMDB0029151","HMDB0011172","HMDB0002022","HMDB0001201","HMDB0001095","HMDB0000663","HMDB0000127","HMDB0000148","HMDB0000641","HMDB0000590","HMDB0000125","HMDB0000125","HMDB0028837","HMDB0001112","HMDB0000139","HMDB0000123","HMDB0028844","HMDB0000721","HMDB0028853","HMDB0001397","HMDB0001273","HMDB0000128","HMDB0000132","HMDB0000133","HMDB0003178","HMDB0246205","HMDB0000870","HMDB0000177","HMDB0028898","HMDB0000670","HMDB0000679","HMDB0000742","HMDB0000719","HMDB0011631","HMDB0240251","HMDB0000965","HMDB0000157","HMDB0002024","HMDB0000175","HMDB0000197","HMDB0000682") 
HMDB_ID_vec <- c(HMDB_ID_vec,"HMDB0000195","NA","HMDB0000172","HMDB0002092","HMDB0000019","HMDB0000695","HMDB0000715","HMDB0000684","HMDB0000190","HMDB0000687","HMDB0000182","HMDB0028964","HMDB0000176","HMDB0000156","HMDB0001389","HMDB0000696","HMDB0062174","HMDB0002005","HMDB0001522","HMDB0002820","HMDB0000202","NA","HMDB0000446","HMDB0002038","HMDB0002172","HMDB0041947","HMDB0001186","HMDB0000206","HMDB0001325","HMDB0002189","HMDB0000766","HMDB0006028","HMDB0000812","HMDB0001067","HMDB0001138","HMDB0006029","HMDB0032055","HMDB0000230","HMDB0003357","HMDB0001238","HMDB0062557","HMDB0013713","HMDB0000902","HMDB0001487","HMDB0304435","HMDB0000221","HMDB0000828","HMDB0255145","HMDB0240317","HMDB0001406","HMDB0000229","HMDB0001488","HMDB0004369","HMDB0004370","HMDB0003011","HMDB0005765","HMDB0000214","HMDB0000226","HMDB0000788","HMDB0255868","NA","HMDB0000210","HMDB0000159","HMDB0001511","HMDB0000263","HMDB0000272","HMDB0002243","HMDB0000716","HMDB0000245","HMDB0000162","HMDB0004827","HMDB0001097","HMDB0000767","HMDB0000802","HMDB0001366","HMDB0001545","HMDB0000239","HMDB0000267","HMDB0000243","HMDB0003072","HMDB0000232","HMDB0000244","HMDB0000867","HMDB0001548","HMDB0000884","HMDB0000279","HMDB0000939","HMDB0001185","HMDB0000797","HMDB0000271","HMDB0258206","HMDB0029032","HMDB0000187","HMDB0000259","HMDB0000126","HMDB0000247","HMDB0001257","HMDB0001256","NA","HMDB0000254","HMDB0001022","HMDB0029416","HMDB0000251","HMDB0000235","HMDB0000943","HMDB0000167","HMDB0000273","HMDB0000262","HMDB0000958","HMDB0000875","HMDB0000925","HMDB0000303","HMDB0000929","HMDB0000158","HMDB0000295","HMDB0000286","HMDB0000935","HMDB0000290","HMDB0000288","HMDB0000300","HMDB0000294","HMDB0000026","HMDB0000289","HMDB0000296","HMDB0000301","HMDB0000285","HMDB0029123","HMDB0029126","HMDB0000883","HMDB0000044","HMDB0000292","HMDB0000299","HMDB0000881","HMDB0001554" )

KEGG_ID_vec <- c("C19806","C07326","C02918","C01152","C16358","C02354","C00108","C01801","C02360","C05984","C02630","C02502","C00322","C00026","C00631","NA","C00575","C00942","C01181","C00882","C03065","NA","C01089","NA","C01188","C03761","C00109","NA","C01152","NA","C00268","C03672","C01179","C00430","C05635","C02376","C03090","C02242","C00024","C00147","C00212","C03794","C00008","C00498","C00301","NA","C03373","NA","NA","C00041","NA","NA","NA","C08265","NA","C00020","C01262","C00062","C03406","NA","C00152","C00049","C00002","C00099","NA","C00486","C00500","C00120","NA","C03017","NA","NA","NA","NA","NA","NA","NA","NA","NA","C00438","C00487","C00386","C00112","C00307","C00570","C00114","NA","C00158","C00327","C00055","C00128","C00010","C00300","C00791","C00063","NA","NA","C01419","C02291","C00506","C05824","NA","C00491","C00475","C00380","C00360","C00239","C00559","C00881","C00330","C05512","C00673","C00526","C00362","C06772","C00415","C00906","C00429","C00111","C03626","C01026","C03758","C00363","C00842","C00364","C00459","C00365","C00460","C06231","NA","C05570","NA","C00016","C04734","C04640","C04376","C00061","C00504","C00439","C00085","C00122","C08348","C00669","C05282","C05283","NA","NA","C03838","C00035","C00325","C00818","C00191","C00025","C00064","NA","C00051","C00051","NA","C00118","C00258","C00037","NA","NA","NA","C00144","C00044","C00581","C00242","C00387","C00032","C02965","C00388","C00135","NA","C01924","C02427","C00155","C00263","C03227","C01015","C00519","C00262","C02835","C00130","C00954","NA","C00294","NA","C00407","C00490","C00141","C00233","C01717","C00328","C00186","C00123","C00047","NA","C01384","C00149","C01598","C00073","NA","NA","C02294","C05828","C02170","NA","C12989","C02728","C03413","NA","C02567","C02727","C03793","C01029","NA","NA","C01042","C12270","C00624","NA","C02997","C19910","C00437","C00978","NA","NA","C00003","C00004","C00006","C00005","NA","NA","NA","C00153","C00455","C00253","C06212","C06213","C00979","NA","C00077","C00295","C01103","C01118","NA","NA","C00079","C02305","C00074","C01005","C10164","C00408","C00931","C00148","C10172","C01079","C02067","C00715","C15587","C00250","C00314","C01879","C00022","C00296","C03722","C00255","C01685","C00117","NA","C00449","C00021","C00019","C04823","C00213","C05382","NA","C00065","C00780","C00093","C00794","C00315","C00750","NA","C00042","C00091","C03884","C00245","C00378","C01620","C00188","C00214","C00178","C02341","C01004","C01104","C00398","C00078","C00082","C00015","C00029","C00167","C00043","C00105","C00106","C00086","C02642","C00366","C00299","C00785","C00075","NA","NA","C00183","C00072","C00385","NA","C02470","C00655") 


universe <- data.frame(
  'Input name' = Input_name_vec,
  'Super class' = Super_class_vec,
  'Main class' = Main_class_vec,
  'Sub class' = Sub_class_vec,
  'HMDB_ID' = HMDB_ID_vec,
  'KEGG_ID' = KEGG_ID_vec,
  stringsAsFactors = FALSE
)


# to make it first get the refmet txt and loadit in r calles universe then 
# a <- unlist(universe$KEGG_ID) |> as.vector()
# writeLines(paste0('"', a, '"', collapse = ","), "input_names.csv")
# open the file with notepad++, copy paste in r

print("#0.3. Set universe finished!")
```

#0.4. Load Data.
```{r}
#1. Load Sample Data
file_listTF <- list.files(input.dir, pattern = ".xlsx", full.names = TRUE, recursive = TRUE) # make a list of xls files in TF folder folder
if(sum(grep("~", file_listTF))>0){file_listTF <- file_listTF[-grep("~", file_listTF)]} # remove any open xls
suppressMessages(data <- readxl::read_excel(file_listTF[grep("ssf", tolower(file_listTF), invert = T)], sheet = 1, col_names = T) |> as.data.frame()) # load data file
rownames(data) <- data[[1]]   # set first column as row names
data <- data[, -1]    # Remove first col

data[] <- lapply(data, function(col) {as.numeric(gsub(" ", "", col)) })# remove spaces and make numeric
if(unique(sapply(data, is.numeric))==FALSE){warning("Input data are NOT numeric!!")} # Check if data are indeed numeric

data[is.na(data)] <- 0 # Set all NAs to zeros


########  ### ### CHANGE DATA ROWNAMES HERE ------------

# Run each row above up to here and then make changes to the raw names so that the ones in the SSF match exactly the rownames of the data.

#stop

#2. Load Sample SSF
suppressMessages(SSF <- readxl::read_excel(file_listTF[grep("ssf", tolower(file_listTF))], sheet = 1, col_names = T) %>% as.data.frame()) # load SSF file
sel.col <- grep("Vial Identifier", SSF, ignore.case = FALSE, value = FALSE)  # find the column we want
sel.row <- grep("Vial Identifier", t(SSF[,grep("Vial Identifier", SSF, ignore.case = FALSE, value = FALSE)]), ignore.case = FALSE, value = FALSE)  # find the row we want
SSF <- SSF[sel.row:length(as.data.frame(t(SSF))),sel.col:ncol(SSF)] # subset the data
if (sum(is.na(SSF[,1])) != 0){  SSF <- SSF[1:min(which(is.na(SSF[,1]))) - 1,]  } # Remove NA rows in the end
colnames(SSF) <- SSF[1,] # make first row into col names
SSF <- SSF[-c(1:4),] # remove the first 4 rows with information
if(sum(grep("Example", SSF[1,]))>0 ){
  SSF <- SSF[-1,] # remove the first 4 rows with information
}
rownames(SSF) <- SSF[,1]  # make 1st column into row names
SSF <- SSF[,-1] # delete fist column
colnames(SSF)[1] <- "Conditions"

SSF$Conditions <- factor(SSF$Conditions, levels = unique(SSF$Conditions))

########  ### ### CHANGE SSF ROWNAMES HERE ---------------


# Run each row above up to here and then make changes to the raw names so that the ones in the SSF match exactly the rownames of the data.

#stop


# Add extra samples in the SSF
# Remove any pools or blanks from the SSF
SSF <- SSF[!grepl("pool", tolower(rownames(SSF)),ignore.case=TRUE ) ,] 
SSF <- SSF[!grepl(blank_samp_id, tolower(rownames(SSF)),ignore.case=TRUE ) ,] 


# Add pool samples back in the SSF
Pool_metadata <- data.frame(matrix(NA, nrow = sum(grepl("Pool",rownames(data),ignore.case=TRUE)), ncol = ncol(SSF))) # Name a dataframe for pool metadata
colnames(Pool_metadata) <- colnames(SSF) # add colnames from metadata
rownames(Pool_metadata) <-  rownames(data)[grepl("Pool", rownames(data),ignore.case=TRUE)] # add rownames of the pool samples from the data
Pool_metadata$Conditions <- rownames(Pool_metadata)
Pool_metadata$Conditions <-   "Pool"


# Add Blank samples back in the SSF
Blank_metadata <- data.frame(matrix(NA, nrow = sum(grepl(blank_samp_id,rownames(data),ignore.case=TRUE)), ncol = ncol(SSF))) # Name a dataframe for pool metadata
colnames(Blank_metadata) <- colnames(SSF) # add colnames from metadata
rownames(Blank_metadata) <-  rownames(data)[grepl(blank_samp_id, rownames(data),ignore.case=TRUE)] # add rownames of the pool samples from the data
Blank_metadata$Conditions <- rownames(Blank_metadata)
Blank_metadata$Conditions <- "Blank"


# Add in a column multiple thingis
# Pool_metadata$`Sample Matrix` <- dplyr::case_when(
#   grepl("Heart", rownames(Pool_metadata), ignore.case = TRUE) ~ "HEART",
#   grepl("Kidney", rownames(Pool_metadata), ignore.case = TRUE) ~ "Kidney",
#   grepl("SI", rownames(Pool_metadata), ignore.case = TRUE) ~ "small intestine",
#   grepl("Muscle", rownames(Pool_metadata), ignore.case = TRUE) ~ "MUSCLE",
#   TRUE ~ "UNKNOWN" # Default value if no match
# )

#  merge the metadata with the pool metadata
SSF <- rbind(SSF, Pool_metadata, Blank_metadata) 
rm(Pool_metadata,Blank_metadata)

# Subset SSF to keep only sample names that exist in the data, as actual sample number might be different from the originally planned.
SSF <- SSF[rownames(data), , drop = FALSE]

# Extract growth vector
if(CoRe==TRUE){
  ssf_for_growth_factor <- SSF %>% select(Conditions, `Cell Number at Time Zero`  ,`Cell Count at Experimental End Point` )
  ssf_for_growth_factor$`Cell Number at Time Zero` <- 1
  ssf_for_growth_factor <- ssf_for_growth_factor[complete.cases(ssf_for_growth_factor),]

  ssf_for_growth_factor[, 2:3] <- lapply(ssf_for_growth_factor[, 2:3], as.numeric)
  growth_factor <- ssf_for_growth_factor %>%
  mutate(GrowthFactor = `Cell Count at Experimental End Point` /
                   `Cell Number at Time Zero` )%>%
  group_by(Conditions) %>%
  summarise(GrowthFactor = unique(GrowthFactor), .groups = "drop")
  
  growth_factor
}

# Keep only columns that are NOT all NA
SSF <- SSF[, colSums(!is.na(SSF)) > 0]


#3. Sample Run Order
file_listRunOrder <- list.files(input.dir, pattern = ".csv",recursive = TRUE, full.names = TRUE) # get all csv files in the raw data folder and subfolders
if(sum(grep("~", file_listRunOrder))>0){file_listRunOrder <- file_listRunOrder[-grep("~", file_listRunOrder)]} # remove open xls

RandomizedSamples <- read.csv(file_listRunOrder) %>% as.data.frame() 
colnames(RandomizedSamples) <- RandomizedSamples[1,] # make first row into col names
RandomizedSamples <- RandomizedSamples[-c(1),] # remove the first 4 rows with information
RandomizedSamples <- RandomizedSamples[,1]



### ### ### Here make changes to the Run Order --------------

# RandomizedSamples[grep(blank_samp_id, RandomizedSamples)][1:length( rownames(data)[grep(blank_samp_id, rownames(data))])] <- rownames(data)[grep(blank_samp_id, rownames(data))]
#stop


RandomizedSamples <- RandomizedSamples[RandomizedSamples %in% rownames(SSF)]

SSF$RunOrder <- match(rownames(SSF), RandomizedSamples)
SSF$RunOrder  <- SSF$RunOrder - min(SSF$RunOrder)+1
rm(RandomizedSamples)


# Remove first 5 pools( Pools that are used to condition the LC column) as they can be incosistent
if(length(grep("PoolB", rownames(data)))>1){
  starting_pools_to_remove <- c("PoolA00", "PoolA01" , "PoolA02")#, "PoolA03" )
}
if(length(grep("PoolB", rownames(data)))>1){
  starting_pools_to_remove <- c("PoolB00", "PoolB01" , "PoolB02")#, "PoolB03" )
}
if(length(grep("PoolC", rownames(data)))>1){
  starting_pools_to_remove <- c("PoolC00", "PoolC01" , "PoolC02")#, "PoolC03" )
}


rownames(data)

data <- data[-grep(paste(starting_pools_to_remove, collapse="|"), rownames(data)),]

# Subset SSF to keep only sample names that exist in the data, as actual sample number might be different from the originally planned.
SSF <- SSF[rownames(data), , drop = FALSE]
# Keep only columns that are NOT all NA
SSF <- SSF[, colSums(!is.na(SSF)) > 0]


SSF_raw <- SSF
data_raw <- data
# Input checks, if all data have the same samples. 
message("All samples in SSF found in data: ", all(rownames(SSF) %in% rownames(data)))
message("All samples in data found in SSF: ", all(rownames(data) %in% rownames(SSF)))

### ----- Check the data ----- ###
# Check the columns that have NAs
check <- data[-grep(blank_samp_id, tolower(rownames(data))),]
Check <- check %>% select( names(check)[colSums(check==0) > 0])

message("There are ",length(colnames(Check)) , " metabolites with missing values." )
# check sample groups
message("Number of pool samples is:", length(grep("pool", tolower(SSF$Conditions))))
message("Number of the smallest sample group is: ",SSF %>% count(Conditions) %>% arrange(n) %>% slice(1)%>% select(n))

print("#0.4. Load Data finished!")
```


#0.5. Set colors.
```{r}

### ---------- Create Custom Colors for plotting ---------- ###
# Check sample groups to select colors.
length(unique(SSF$Conditions) )
unique(SSF$Conditions)

# Create customColors table
customColors <- data.frame(
  Conditions = unique(SSF$Conditions),
  # colors <- c("red", "blue", "green", "orange", "purple", "cyan", "magenta","white, "grey")[1:length(unique(SSF$Conditions))]
  Color = c( "#8eb841", "red", "blue", "grey","black", "#3c4f1b", "red","black","lightblue", "blue", "darkblue", "#bdd78d", "#8eb841", "#3c4f1b", "red" ,"grey")[1:length(unique(SSF$Conditions))]
)
customColorsBox <- customColors

# Map colors to SSF without merging
SSF$Color <- customColors$Color[match(SSF$Conditions, customColors$Conditions)]
SSF_raw <- SSF

# Set the Media_sample and their controls.
if(CoRe==T){
  media_sample_names <- rownames(SSF)[-grep("Ctrl", rownames(SSF))]
  media_control_names <- c(rownames(SSF)[grep("Ctrl", rownames(SSF))],rownames(SSF)[grep("Blank", rownames(SSF))])
}
print("#0.5. Set colors!")
```

#0.6. Load helper functions.
```{r}
# res <- make_pca_grid(data, SSF, out_dir = Data_cleaning_dir)
make_pca_grid <- function(data, SSF,
                          add_to_name = NULL,
                          project_code = ProjectCode,
                          color_col = "Conditions",
                          fig_size_cm = c(60, 40)) {
  
  suppressMessages(suppressWarnings({
    
    PCA_dir <- file.path(output.dir, "PCA_plots")
    if (!dir.exists(PCA_dir)) dir.create(PCA_dir, recursive = TRUE)
    
    # labels
    if (!is.null(project_code)) {
      SSF$Label <- sub(paste0("^", project_code, "_"), "", rownames(SSF))
    } else {
      SSF$Label <- rownames(SSF)
    }
    
    # PCA input: copy, fill NAs with 0, drop zero-variance features
    X <- data
    X[is.na(X)] <- 0
    nzv <- apply(X, 2, sd) > 0
    if (!all(nzv)) X <- X[, nzv, drop = FALSE]
    if (ncol(X) < 2) stop("Not enough non-zero-variance features for PCA.")
    
    # PCA
    pca_res <- prcomp(X, scale. = TRUE)
    n_pc_use <- min(5, ncol(pca_res$x))
    
    # palette from SSF if available
    pal <- NULL
    if ("Color" %in% colnames(SSF) && color_col %in% colnames(SSF)) {
      pal_df <- unique(SSF[, c(color_col, "Color")])
      pal <- setNames(pal_df$Color, pal_df[[color_col]])
    }
    
    # builder
    build_grid <- function(label_points = FALSE) {
      plots <- vector("list", n_pc_use * n_pc_use)
      k <- 1
      for (i in seq_len(n_pc_use)) {
        for (j in seq_len(n_pc_use)) {
          if (i != j) {
            p <- autoplot(
              pca_res, data = SSF, x = i, y = j,
              colour = color_col, size = 2, alpha = 0.8, loadings = FALSE
            ) +
              theme_classic() +
              ggtitle(paste("PC", i, "vs PC", j)) +
              geom_hline(yintercept = 0, color = "black", linewidth = 0.1) +
              geom_vline(xintercept = 0, color = "black", linewidth = 0.1)
            if (!is.null(pal)) p <- p + scale_color_manual(values = pal)
            if (label_points) p <- p + ggrepel::geom_text_repel(aes(label = Label), size = 2.5)
          } else {
            p <- ggplot() + theme_void() + ggtitle(paste0("PC", i, " vs ", j))
          }
          plots[[k]] <- p; k <- k + 1
        }
      }
      patchwork::wrap_plots(plots, ncol = n_pc_use)
    }
    
    # build + save
    p_plain   <- build_grid(FALSE)
    p_labeled <- build_grid(TRUE)
    
    ggsave(file.path(PCA_dir, paste0(add_to_name, "__PCA.svg")),
           plot = p_plain, width = fig_size_cm[1], height = fig_size_cm[2], units = "cm")
    ggsave(file.path(PCA_dir,  paste0(add_to_name, "__PCA_Labeled.svg")),
           plot = p_labeled, width = fig_size_cm[1], height = fig_size_cm[2], units = "cm")
    
    invisible(list(plain = p_plain, labeled = p_labeled, pca = pca_res))
  }))
}

make_signal_drift_reports <- function(data, SSF,
                                      add_to_name = NULL,
                                      project_code = ProjectCode,
                                      valine_pattern = valine_pattern_id) {
  
  # silence warnings/messages inside this function
  old_warn <- getOption("warn"); options(warn = -1)
  on.exit(options(warn = old_warn), add = TRUE)
  
  suppressMessages(suppressWarnings({
    
    SignalDrift_dir <- file.path(output.dir, "SignalDrift")
    if (!dir.exists(SignalDrift_dir)) dir.create(SignalDrift_dir, recursive = TRUE)
    
    # Palette from SSF
    pal_df <- unique(SSF[, c("Conditions","Color")])
    customColors <- setNames(pal_df$Color, pal_df$Conditions)
    
    # Pool label (character)
    v_cond <- as.character(SSF$Conditions)
    pool_label <- {
      u <- unique(v_cond[grepl("pool", tolower(v_cond))])
      if (length(u)) u[1] else NA_character_
    }
    
    # Order by RunOrder
    valid_samples <- rownames(SSF)[order(SSF$RunOrder)]
    
    # ---- Prepare Drift Data ----
    Drift_data <- merge(SSF %>% dplyr::select(Conditions), data, by = 0, sort = FALSE)
    colnames(Drift_data)[1] <- "File.Name"
    rownames(Drift_data) <- Drift_data$File.Name
    
    Drift_data_ordered <- Drift_data[match(valid_samples, Drift_data$File.Name), , drop = FALSE]
    Drift_data_ordered <- Drift_data_ordered[!is.na(rownames(Drift_data_ordered)), ]
    Drift_data_ordered$File.Name <- NULL
    Drift_data_ordered[, -1] <- sapply(Drift_data_ordered[, -1], as.numeric)
    
    row_medians <- matrixStats::rowMedians(as.matrix(Drift_data_ordered[, -1]), na.rm = TRUE)
    row_sums    <- rowSums(as.matrix(Drift_data_ordered[, -1]), na.rm = TRUE)
    
    plotdata <- data.frame(SampleMedian = row_medians,
                           TIC = row_sums,
                           SampleGroup = Drift_data_ordered$Conditions) %>%
      tibble::rownames_to_column("RowNames")
    plotdata$RowNames <- factor(plotdata$RowNames, levels = plotdata$RowNames)
    plotdata$Order <- seq_len(nrow(plotdata))
    
    # palette for used groups
    pal_for <- function(groups) {
      used <- intersect(names(customColors), unique(as.character(groups)))
      customColors[used]
    }
    
    # file prefix
    prefix <- if (!is.null(add_to_name) && nzchar(add_to_name)) paste0(add_to_name, "__") else ""
    
    # ---- Plot helper ----
    make_drift_plot <- function(df, yvar, ylab, title, filename) {
      pal_use <- pal_for(df$SampleGroup)
      df$SampleGroup <- factor(as.character(df$SampleGroup), levels = names(pal_use))
      pool_df <- if (!is.na(pool_label)) df[as.character(df$SampleGroup) == pool_label, , drop = FALSE] else NULL
      
      p <- ggplot(df, aes(x = RowNames, y = .data[[yvar]], fill = SampleGroup)) +
        geom_col(position = "dodge") +
        geom_smooth(
          data = pool_df,
          aes(x = Order, y = .data[[yvar]]),
          method = "lm", se = FALSE, color = "red", inherit.aes = FALSE
        ) +
        scale_fill_manual(values = pal_use, drop = FALSE) +
        labs(title = title, x = "Samples by acquirement order", y = ylab) +
        theme_minimal() +
        theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 40, hjust = 1),
              plot.title = element_text(size = 22))
      
      ggsave(file.path(SignalDrift_dir, paste0(prefix, filename)),
             plot = p, width = 10, height = 8)
    }
    
    # ---- Signal Drift Plots ----
    make_drift_plot(plotdata, "SampleMedian", "Sample Medians",
                    "Signal drift, Sample Median", "SignalDrift_Median_SampleGroup.svg")
    make_drift_plot(plotdata, "TIC", "Sample TIC",
                    "Signal drift, TIC", "SignalDrift_TIC_SampleGroup.svg")
    
    plotdata_pool <- transform(plotdata,
                               SampleGroup = if (!is.na(pool_label)) ifelse(SampleGroup == pool_label, pool_label, "Sample") else "Sample")
    make_drift_plot(plotdata_pool, "SampleMedian", "Sample Medians",
                    "Signal drift, Median, Pool Samples", "SignalDrift_Median_PoolSamples.svg")
    make_drift_plot(plotdata_pool, "TIC", "Sample TIC",
                    "Signal drift, TIC, Pool Samples", "SignalDrift_TIC_PoolSamples.svg")
    
    # ---- Valine-d8 block (optional; runs only if found) ----
    valine_idx <- grep(valine_pattern, tolower(colnames(Drift_data_ordered)), ignore.case = TRUE)
    valine_done <- FALSE
    
    if (length(valine_idx) > 0) {
      valine_col <- colnames(Drift_data_ordered)[valine_idx[1]]
      plotdataIS <- data.frame(
        ValineIS    = Drift_data_ordered[[valine_col]],
        SampleGroup = Drift_data_ordered$Conditions,
        RowNames    = rownames(Drift_data_ordered),
        check.names = FALSE
      )
      plotdataIS$Index <- seq_len(nrow(plotdataIS))
      plotdataIS$RowNames <- factor(plotdataIS$RowNames,
                                    levels = rownames(Drift_data_ordered), ordered = TRUE)
      pal_use <- pal_for(plotdataIS$SampleGroup)
      
      val_cv <- function(x) round(100 * sd(x, na.rm = TRUE) / mean(x, na.rm = TRUE), 2)
      ValSampCV <- val_cv(plotdataIS$ValineIS)
      ValPoolCV <- if (!is.na(pool_label)) val_cv(plotdataIS$ValineIS[plotdataIS$SampleGroup == pool_label]) else NA_real_
      
      p_val <- ggplot(plotdataIS, aes(x = RowNames, y = ValineIS, fill = SampleGroup)) +
        geom_col(position = "dodge") +
        geom_smooth(aes(x = Index, y = ValineIS), method = "lm", se = FALSE, color = "red", inherit.aes = FALSE) +
        scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, NA)) +
        scale_fill_manual(values = pal_use, drop = FALSE) +
        labs(title = paste0("Signal drift, ", valine_col, " (IS)"),
             subtitle = paste("PoolCV =", ValPoolCV, ", SampleCV =", ValSampCV),
             x = "Samples by acquirement order", y = "Peak Area") +
        theme_minimal() +
        theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 40, hjust = 1),
              plot.title = element_text(size = 22))
      ggsave(file.path(SignalDrift_dir, paste0(add_to_name, "_SignalDrift_Valine-d8(IS).svg")),
             plot = p_val, width = 12, height = 10)
      
      # Correlations
      plotdata_combined <- merge(plotdata, plotdataIS %>% dplyr::select(-SampleGroup), by = "RowNames")
      cor_TIC_valine    <- cor(plotdata_combined$TIC,          plotdata_combined$ValineIS, use = "complete.obs")
      cor_median_valine <- cor(plotdata_combined$SampleMedian, plotdata_combined$ValineIS, use = "complete.obs")
      
      # Extraction Buffer (optional)
      eb_col <- grep("^Extraction\\s*Buffer$", names(SSF), ignore.case = TRUE, value = TRUE)
      if (length(eb_col) == 1) {
        plotdata_combined$ExtractionBuffer <- SSF[match(plotdata_combined$RowNames, rownames(SSF)), eb_col]
        plotdata_combined$ExtractionBuffer <- as.numeric(plotdata_combined$ExtractionBuffer)
        plotdata_combined$ExtractionBuffer[is.na(plotdata_combined$ExtractionBuffer)] <-
          mean(plotdata_combined$ExtractionBuffer, na.rm = TRUE)
        cor_ExtrBuffer_valine <- cor(plotdata_combined$ExtractionBuffer, plotdata_combined$ValineIS, use = "complete.obs")
      } else {
        plotdata_combined$ExtractionBuffer <- NA_real_
        cor_ExtrBuffer_valine <- NA_real_
      }
      
      p1 <- ggplot(plotdata_combined, aes(x = ValineIS, y = TIC, color = SampleGroup)) +
        geom_point(size = 3) + geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = FALSE, color = "black") +
        scale_color_manual(values = pal_for(plotdata_combined$SampleGroup), drop = FALSE) +
        labs(title = paste("TIC vs", valine_col, "(r =", round(cor_TIC_valine, 3), ")"),
             x = paste(valine_col, "(IS Peak Area)"), y = "TIC") + theme_minimal()
      
      p2 <- ggplot(plotdata_combined, aes(x = ValineIS, y = SampleMedian, color = SampleGroup)) +
        geom_point(size = 3) + geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = FALSE, color = "black") +
        scale_color_manual(values = pal_for(plotdata_combined$SampleGroup), drop = FALSE) +
        labs(title = paste("Sample Median vs", valine_col, "(r =", round(cor_median_valine, 3), ")"),
             x = paste(valine_col, "(IS Peak Area)"), y = "Sample Median") + theme_minimal()
      
      p3 <- ggplot(plotdata_combined, aes(x = ValineIS, y = ExtractionBuffer, color = SampleGroup)) +
        geom_point(size = 3) + geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = FALSE, color = "black") +
        scale_color_manual(values = pal_for(plotdata_combined$SampleGroup), drop = FALSE) +
        labs(title = paste("Extraction Buffer vs", valine_col, "(r =", round(cor_ExtrBuffer_valine, 3), ")"),
             x = paste(valine_col, "(IS Peak Area)"), y = "Extraction Buffer") + theme_minimal()
      
      plotdata_combined[plotdata_combined==0] <- 1
      cor_matrix <- plotdata_combined %>%
        dplyr::select(TIC, SampleMedian, ValineIS) %>% as.data.frame() %>%
        stats::cor(use = "complete.obs")
      
      cor_df <- reshape2::melt(cor_matrix)
      colnames(cor_df) <- c("Var1", "Var2", "Correlation")
      
      p4 <- ggplot(cor_df, aes(x = Var1, y = Var2, fill = Correlation)) +
        geom_tile(color = "white") +
        geom_text(aes(label = round(Correlation, 2)), color = "black", size = 5) +
        scale_fill_gradient2(low = "blue", high = "red", mid = "white", midpoint = 0, limit = c(-1, 1)) +
        theme_minimal() + labs(title = "Correlation Matrix") +
        theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1))
      
      p_all <- (p1 | p2 | p3 | p4)
      ggsave(file.path(SignalDrift_dir, paste0(add_to_name, "_Correlation_TIC_Median_ValineIS.svg")),
             plot = p_all, width = 24, height = 6)
      
      # Feature-wise correlations
      valine_vector <- Drift_data_ordered[, valine_col]
      valine_vector <- valine_vector[match(rownames(data), rownames(Drift_data_ordered))]
      
      feature_cor <- sapply(data, function(v) cor(v, valine_vector , use = "complete.obs"))
      feature_cor_df <- data.frame(Feature = names(feature_cor),
                                   CorrelationWithValineIS = feature_cor) %>%
        dplyr::arrange(CorrelationWithValineIS)
      
      top_neg <- head(feature_cor_df[order(feature_cor_df$CorrelationWithValineIS), ], 20) %>%
        dplyr::mutate(Feature = gsub("\\*", "", Feature))
      top_pos <- tail(feature_cor_df[order(feature_cor_df$CorrelationWithValineIS), ], 20) %>%
        dplyr::mutate(Feature = gsub("\\*", "", Feature))
      
      if (exists("ProjectFolder")) {
        rt_file <- file.path(ProjectFolder, "tsatsiki_v03_media/FINAL_TF_LIST.xlsx")
        if (file.exists(rt_file) && requireNamespace("readxl", quietly = TRUE)) {
          featRTs <- readxl::read_excel(rt_file)
          top_neg <- dplyr::left_join(top_neg, featRTs %>% dplyr::select(`Standardized name`, RT),
                                      by = c("Feature" = "Standardized name")) %>%
            dplyr::mutate(Feature = paste0(Feature, " (RT=", round(RT, 2), ")"))
          top_pos <- dplyr::left_join(top_pos, featRTs %>% dplyr::select(`Standardized name`, RT),
                                      by = c("Feature" = "Standardized name")) %>%
            dplyr::mutate(Feature = paste0(Feature, " (RT=", round(RT, 2), ")"))
        }
      }
      
      p_hist <- ggplot(feature_cor_df, aes(x = CorrelationWithValineIS)) +
        geom_histogram(bins = 50, fill = "steelblue", color = "white") +
        labs(title = paste("Feature-wise Correlation with", valine_col),
             x = "Pearson Correlation", y = "Feature Count") +
        theme_minimal()
      
      p_neg <- ggplot(top_neg, aes(x = reorder(Feature, CorrelationWithValineIS),
                                   y = CorrelationWithValineIS)) +
        geom_col(fill = "firebrick") + coord_flip() +
        labs(title = "Top 20 Most Negatively Correlated Features",
             x = "Feature", y = "Correlation") + theme_minimal()
      
      p_pos <- ggplot(top_pos, aes(x = reorder(Feature, CorrelationWithValineIS),
                                   y = CorrelationWithValineIS)) +
        geom_col(fill = "darkgreen") + coord_flip() +
        labs(title = "Top 20 Most Positively Correlated Features",
             x = "Feature", y = "Correlation") + theme_minimal()
      
      combined_plot <- p_hist / (p_neg | p_pos)
      ggsave(file.path(SignalDrift_dir, paste0(add_to_name, "_Feature_wise_Valine-d8_Correlations.svg")),
             plot = combined_plot, width = 18, height = 10)
      
      valine_done <- TRUE
    } else {
      valine_done <- FALSE
    }
    
    invisible(list(
      outdir = SignalDrift_dir,
      pool_label = pool_label,
      valine_found = valine_done
    ))
  }))
}
print("#0.6. Load helper functions!")
```

```{r}
# ───────────────────────────────────────────────
### HELPER FUNCTIONS: PCA + SIGNAL DRIFT PLOTS --


# PCA grid (pairwise PC plots + labeled version)
make_pca_grid <- function(data, SSF,
                          add_to_name   = NULL,
                          project_code  = ProjectCode,
                          color_col     = "Conditions",
                          fig_size_cm   = c(60, 40)) {

  suppressMessages(suppressWarnings({

    # ── Output folder ───────────────────────────
    PCA_dir <- file.path(output.dir, "PCA_plots")
    if (!dir.exists(PCA_dir)) dir.create(PCA_dir, recursive = TRUE)

    # ── Labels (remove leading "ProjectCode_" if present) ───────────────
    if (!is.null(project_code) && nzchar(project_code)) {
      SSF$Label <- sub(paste0("^", project_code, "_"), "", rownames(SSF))
    } else {
      SSF$Label <- rownames(SSF)
    }

    # ── PCA input (safe) ────────────────────────
    X <- data
    X[is.na(X)] <- 0

    # Drop zero-variance features
    nzv <- apply(X, 2, sd, na.rm = TRUE) > 0
    if (!all(nzv)) X <- X[, nzv, drop = FALSE]
    if (ncol(X) < 2) stop("Not enough non-zero-variance features for PCA.")

    # ── Run PCA ─────────────────────────────────
    pca_res  <- prcomp(X, scale. = TRUE)
    n_pc_use <- min(5, ncol(pca_res$x))

    # ── Palette from SSF (if Color column exists) ───────────────────────
    pal <- NULL
    if ("Color" %in% colnames(SSF) && color_col %in% colnames(SSF)) {
      pal_df <- unique(SSF[, c(color_col, "Color")])
      pal <- setNames(pal_df$Color, pal_df[[color_col]])
    }

    # ── Plot builder ────────────────────────────
    build_grid <- function(label_points = FALSE) {

      plots <- vector("list", n_pc_use * n_pc_use)
      k <- 1

      for (i in seq_len(n_pc_use)) {
        for (j in seq_len(n_pc_use)) {

          if (i != j) {

            p <- autoplot(
              pca_res, data = SSF, x = i, y = j,
              colour = color_col, size = 2, alpha = 0.8, loadings = FALSE
            ) +
              theme_classic() +
              ggtitle(paste("PC", i, "vs PC", j)) +
              geom_hline(yintercept = 0, color = "black", linewidth = 0.1) +
              geom_vline(xintercept = 0, color = "black", linewidth = 0.1)

            if (!is.null(pal)) p <- p + scale_color_manual(values = pal)
            if (label_points) {
              p <- p + ggrepel::geom_text_repel(aes(label = Label), size = 2.5)
            }

          } else {
            # diagonal cell: keep empty to make the grid readable
            p <- ggplot() + theme_void()
          }

          plots[[k]] <- p
          k <- k + 1
        }
      }

      patchwork::wrap_plots(plots, ncol = n_pc_use)
    }

    # ── Build + save ────────────────────────────
    prefix <- if (!is.null(add_to_name) && nzchar(add_to_name)) paste0(add_to_name, "__") else ""

    p_plain   <- build_grid(FALSE)
    p_labeled <- build_grid(TRUE)

    ggsave(
      filename = file.path(PCA_dir, paste0(prefix, "PCA.svg")),
      plot = p_plain, width = fig_size_cm[1], height = fig_size_cm[2], units = "cm"
    )

    ggsave(
      filename = file.path(PCA_dir, paste0(prefix, "PCA_Labeled.svg")),
      plot = p_labeled, width = fig_size_cm[1], height = fig_size_cm[2], units = "cm"
    )

    invisible(list(plain = p_plain, labeled = p_labeled, pca = pca_res))
  }))
}


# Signal drift reports (median + TIC, with optional Valine-d8 diagnostics)
make_signal_drift_reports <- function(data, SSF,
                                      add_to_name     = NULL,
                                      project_code    = ProjectCode,
                                      valine_pattern  = valine_pattern_id) {

  # Silence warnings inside (without changing global state permanently)
  old_warn <- getOption("warn")
  options(warn = -1)
  on.exit(options(warn = old_warn), add = TRUE)

  suppressMessages(suppressWarnings({

    # ── Output folder ───────────────────────────
    SignalDrift_dir <- file.path(output.dir, "SignalDrift")
    if (!dir.exists(SignalDrift_dir)) dir.create(SignalDrift_dir, recursive = TRUE)

    # ── Palette from SSF ────────────────────────
    pal_df <- unique(SSF[, c("Conditions", "Color")])
    customColors <- setNames(pal_df$Color, pal_df$Conditions)

    pal_for <- function(groups) {
      used <- intersect(names(customColors), unique(as.character(groups)))
      customColors[used]
    }

    # ── Pool label (first condition containing "pool") ──────────────────
    v_cond <- as.character(SSF$Conditions)
    pool_label <- {
      u <- unique(v_cond[grepl("pool", tolower(v_cond))])
      if (length(u)) u[1] else NA_character_
    }

    # ── Order by RunOrder ───────────────────────
    valid_samples <- rownames(SSF)[order(SSF$RunOrder)]

    # ── Merge Conditions + data (keeps rownames) ────────────────────────
    Drift_data <- merge(SSF %>% dplyr::select(Conditions), data, by = 0, sort = FALSE)
    colnames(Drift_data)[1] <- "File.Name"
    rownames(Drift_data) <- Drift_data$File.Name

    # Enforce acquisition order
    Drift_data_ordered <- Drift_data[match(valid_samples, Drift_data$File.Name), , drop = FALSE]
    Drift_data_ordered <- Drift_data_ordered[!is.na(rownames(Drift_data_ordered)), ]
    Drift_data_ordered$File.Name <- NULL

    # Ensure numeric intensities
    Drift_data_ordered[, -1] <- sapply(Drift_data_ordered[, -1], as.numeric)

    # ── Compute drift metrics (Median + TIC) ────────────────────────────
    row_medians <- matrixStats::rowMedians(as.matrix(Drift_data_ordered[, -1]), na.rm = TRUE)
    row_sums    <- rowSums(as.matrix(Drift_data_ordered[, -1]), na.rm = TRUE)

    plotdata <- data.frame(
      SampleMedian = row_medians,
      TIC          = row_sums,
      SampleGroup  = Drift_data_ordered$Conditions,
      check.names  = FALSE
    ) %>% tibble::rownames_to_column("RowNames")

    plotdata$RowNames <- factor(plotdata$RowNames, levels = plotdata$RowNames)
    plotdata$Order    <- seq_len(nrow(plotdata))

    # filename prefix
    prefix <- if (!is.null(add_to_name) && nzchar(add_to_name)) paste0(add_to_name, "__") else ""

    # ── Plot helper ────────────────────────────
    make_drift_plot <- function(df, yvar, ylab, title, filename) {

      pal_use <- pal_for(df$SampleGroup)
      df$SampleGroup <- factor(as.character(df$SampleGroup), levels = names(pal_use))

      pool_df <- if (!is.na(pool_label)) {
        df[as.character(df$SampleGroup) == pool_label, , drop = FALSE]
      } else {
        NULL
      }

      p <- ggplot(df, aes(x = RowNames, y = .data[[yvar]], fill = SampleGroup)) +
        geom_col(position = "dodge") +
        geom_smooth(
          data = pool_df,
          aes(x = Order, y = .data[[yvar]]),
          method = "lm", se = FALSE,
          color = "red", inherit.aes = FALSE
        ) +
        scale_fill_manual(values = pal_use, drop = FALSE) +
        labs(title = title, x = "Samples by acquisition order", y = ylab) +
        theme_minimal() +
        theme(
          axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 40, hjust = 1),
          plot.title  = element_text(size = 22)
        )

      ggsave(
        filename = file.path(SignalDrift_dir, paste0(prefix, filename)),
        plot = p, width = 10, height = 8
      )
    }

    # ── Core drift plots ────────────────────────
    make_drift_plot(plotdata, "SampleMedian", "Sample median",
                    "Signal drift: sample median", "SignalDrift_Median_SampleGroup.svg")
    make_drift_plot(plotdata, "TIC", "Total ion count (TIC)",
                    "Signal drift: TIC", "SignalDrift_TIC_SampleGroup.svg")

    # Pool vs Sample (binary view)
    plotdata_pool <- transform(
      plotdata,
      SampleGroup = if (!is.na(pool_label)) ifelse(SampleGroup == pool_label, pool_label, "Sample") else "Sample"
    )

    make_drift_plot(plotdata_pool, "SampleMedian", "Sample median",
                    "Signal drift: median (pool highlighted)", "SignalDrift_Median_PoolSamples.svg")
    make_drift_plot(plotdata_pool, "TIC", "Total ion count (TIC)",
                    "Signal drift: TIC (pool highlighted)", "SignalDrift_TIC_PoolSamples.svg")

    # ── Optional: Valine-d8 diagnostics ─────────
    valine_idx  <- grep(valine_pattern, tolower(colnames(Drift_data_ordered)), ignore.case = TRUE)
    valine_done <- FALSE

    if (length(valine_idx) > 0) {

      valine_col <- colnames(Drift_data_ordered)[valine_idx[1]]

      plotdataIS <- data.frame(
        ValineIS    = Drift_data_ordered[[valine_col]],
        SampleGroup = Drift_data_ordered$Conditions,
        RowNames    = rownames(Drift_data_ordered),
        check.names = FALSE
      )

      plotdataIS$Index   <- seq_len(nrow(plotdataIS))
      plotdataIS$RowNames <- factor(plotdataIS$RowNames,
                                    levels = rownames(Drift_data_ordered), ordered = TRUE)

      pal_use <- pal_for(plotdataIS$SampleGroup)

      val_cv <- function(x) round(100 * sd(x, na.rm = TRUE) / mean(x, na.rm = TRUE), 2)
      ValSampCV <- val_cv(plotdataIS$ValineIS)
      ValPoolCV <- if (!is.na(pool_label)) val_cv(plotdataIS$ValineIS[plotdataIS$SampleGroup == pool_label]) else NA_real_

      # Valine drift plot
      p_val <- ggplot(plotdataIS, aes(x = RowNames, y = ValineIS, fill = SampleGroup)) +
        geom_col(position = "dodge") +
        geom_smooth(aes(x = Index, y = ValineIS),
                    method = "lm", se = FALSE, color = "red", inherit.aes = FALSE) +
        scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, NA)) +
        scale_fill_manual(values = pal_use, drop = FALSE) +
        labs(
          title    = paste0("Signal drift: ", valine_col, " (internal standard)"),
          subtitle = paste("PoolCV =", ValPoolCV, "| SampleCV =", ValSampCV),
          x = "Samples by acquisition order", y = "Peak area"
        ) +
        theme_minimal() +
        theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 40, hjust = 1),
              plot.title  = element_text(size = 22))

      ggsave(
        filename = file.path(SignalDrift_dir, paste0(prefix, "SignalDrift_Valine-d8(IS).svg")),
        plot = p_val, width = 12, height = 10
      )

      # Combine metrics for correlations
      plotdata_combined <- merge(plotdata, plotdataIS %>% dplyr::select(-SampleGroup), by = "RowNames")

      cor_TIC_valine    <- cor(plotdata_combined$TIC,          plotdata_combined$ValineIS, use = "complete.obs")
      cor_median_valine <- cor(plotdata_combined$SampleMedian, plotdata_combined$ValineIS, use = "complete.obs")

      # Optional: Extraction Buffer vs IS
      eb_col <- grep("^Extraction\\s*Buffer$", names(SSF), ignore.case = TRUE, value = TRUE)

      if (length(eb_col) == 1) {
        plotdata_combined$ExtractionBuffer <- SSF[match(plotdata_combined$RowNames, rownames(SSF)), eb_col]
        plotdata_combined$ExtractionBuffer <- as.numeric(plotdata_combined$ExtractionBuffer)
        plotdata_combined$ExtractionBuffer[is.na(plotdata_combined$ExtractionBuffer)] <-
          mean(plotdata_combined$ExtractionBuffer, na.rm = TRUE)

        ok <- complete.cases(plotdata_combined$ExtractionBuffer, plotdata_combined$ValineIS)
        cor_ExtrBuffer_valine <- if (sum(ok) >= 2) {
          cor(plotdata_combined$ExtractionBuffer[ok], plotdata_combined$ValineIS[ok])
        } else {
          NA_real_
        }
      } else {
        plotdata_combined$ExtractionBuffer <- NA_real_
        cor_ExtrBuffer_valine <- NA_real_
      }

      # Scatter plots
      p1 <- ggplot(plotdata_combined, aes(x = ValineIS, y = TIC, color = SampleGroup)) +
        geom_point(size = 3) +
        geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = FALSE, color = "black") +
        scale_color_manual(values = pal_for(plotdata_combined$SampleGroup), drop = FALSE) +
        labs(
          title = paste("TIC vs", valine_col, "(r =", round(cor_TIC_valine, 3), ")"),
          x = paste(valine_col, "(IS peak area)"), y = "TIC"
        ) +
        theme_minimal()

      p2 <- ggplot(plotdata_combined, aes(x = ValineIS, y = SampleMedian, color = SampleGroup)) +
        geom_point(size = 3) +
        geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = FALSE, color = "black") +
        scale_color_manual(values = pal_for(plotdata_combined$SampleGroup), drop = FALSE) +
        labs(
          title = paste("Median vs", valine_col, "(r =", round(cor_median_valine, 3), ")"),
          x = paste(valine_col, "(IS peak area)"), y = "Sample median"
        ) +
        theme_minimal()

      p3 <- ggplot(plotdata_combined, aes(x = ValineIS, y = ExtractionBuffer, color = SampleGroup)) +
        geom_point(size = 3) +
        geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = FALSE, color = "black") +
        scale_color_manual(values = pal_for(plotdata_combined$SampleGroup), drop = FALSE) +
        labs(
          title = paste("Extraction buffer vs", valine_col, "(r =", round(cor_ExtrBuffer_valine, 3), ")"),
          x = paste(valine_col, "(IS peak area)"), y = "Extraction buffer"
        ) +
        theme_minimal()

      # Correlation heatmap (TIC / Median / IS)
      cor_matrix <- plotdata_combined %>%
        dplyr::select(TIC, SampleMedian, ValineIS) %>%
        as.data.frame() %>%
        stats::cor(use = "complete.obs")

      cor_df <- reshape2::melt(cor_matrix)
      colnames(cor_df) <- c("Var1", "Var2", "Correlation")

      p4 <- ggplot(cor_df, aes(x = Var1, y = Var2, fill = Correlation)) +
        geom_tile(color = "white") +
        geom_text(aes(label = round(Correlation, 2)), color = "black", size = 5) +
        scale_fill_gradient2(low = "blue", high = "red", mid = "white", midpoint = 0, limit = c(-1, 1)) +
        theme_minimal() +
        labs(title = "Correlation matrix") +
        theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1))

      p_all <- (p1 | p2 | p3 | p4)

      ggsave(
        filename = file.path(SignalDrift_dir, paste0(prefix, "Correlation_TIC_Median_ValineIS.svg")),
        plot = p_all, width = 24, height = 6
      )

      # Feature-wise correlations vs IS
      valine_vector <- Drift_data_ordered[, valine_col]
      valine_vector <- valine_vector[match(rownames(data), rownames(Drift_data_ordered))]

      feature_cor <- sapply(data, function(v) cor(v, valine_vector, use = "complete.obs"))
      feature_cor_df <- data.frame(
        Feature = names(feature_cor),
        CorrelationWithValineIS = feature_cor,
        check.names = FALSE
      ) %>% dplyr::arrange(CorrelationWithValineIS)

      top_neg <- head(feature_cor_df[order(feature_cor_df$CorrelationWithValineIS), ], 20) %>%
        dplyr::mutate(Feature = gsub("\\*", "", Feature))
      top_pos <- tail(feature_cor_df[order(feature_cor_df$CorrelationWithValineIS), ], 20) %>%
        dplyr::mutate(Feature = gsub("\\*", "", Feature))

      # Optional: annotate with RT if TF list exists
      if (exists("ProjectFolder")) {
        rt_file <- file.path(ProjectFolder, "tsatsiki_v03_media/FINAL_TF_LIST.xlsx")
        if (file.exists(rt_file) && requireNamespace("readxl", quietly = TRUE)) {
          featRTs <- readxl::read_excel(rt_file)

          top_neg <- dplyr::left_join(top_neg, featRTs %>% dplyr::select(`Standardized name`, RT),
                                      by = c("Feature" = "Standardized name")) %>%
            dplyr::mutate(Feature = paste0(Feature, " (RT=", round(RT, 2), ")"))

          top_pos <- dplyr::left_join(top_pos, featRTs %>% dplyr::select(`Standardized name`, RT),
                                      by = c("Feature" = "Standardized name")) %>%
            dplyr::mutate(Feature = paste0(Feature, " (RT=", round(RT, 2), ")"))
        }
      }

      p_hist <- ggplot(feature_cor_df, aes(x = CorrelationWithValineIS)) +
        geom_histogram(bins = 50, fill = "steelblue", color = "white") +
        labs(title = paste("Feature-wise correlation with", valine_col),
             x = "Pearson correlation", y = "Number of features") +
        theme_minimal()

      p_neg <- ggplot(top_neg, aes(x = reorder(Feature, CorrelationWithValineIS), y = CorrelationWithValineIS)) +
        geom_col(fill = "firebrick") +
        coord_flip() +
        labs(title = "Top 20 most negatively correlated features",
             x = "Feature", y = "Correlation") +
        theme_minimal()

      p_pos <- ggplot(top_pos, aes(x = reorder(Feature, CorrelationWithValineIS), y = CorrelationWithValineIS)) +
        geom_col(fill = "darkgreen") +
        coord_flip() +
        labs(title = "Top 20 most positively correlated features",
             x = "Feature", y = "Correlation") +
        theme_minimal()

      combined_plot <- p_hist / (p_neg | p_pos)

      ggsave(
        filename = file.path(SignalDrift_dir, paste0(prefix, "Feature_wise_Valine-d8_Correlations.svg")),
        plot = combined_plot, width = 18, height = 10
      )

      valine_done <- TRUE
    }

    invisible(list(
      outdir        = SignalDrift_dir,
      pool_label    = pool_label,
      valine_found  = valine_done
    ))
  }))
}


# ──────────────────────────────────────────────
# Output message
message("#0.6. Load helper functions — completed successfully.")

```

### ---- 1. Data Cleaning ---- 
# 1.0 Preparation.
```{r}
# Set sub folders
Data_cleaning_dir <- file.path(output.dir, "1. Data Cleaning")
if (!dir.exists(Data_cleaning_dir)) dir.create(Data_cleaning_dir, recursive = TRUE)

Metabolite_Metadata <- data.frame(matrix(ncol = 0, nrow = length(colnames(data))))
Metabolite_Metadata$Metabolite <- colnames(data)
# Metabolite_Metadata$`SNratio<3` <- rownames(Metabolite_Metadata) %in% gsub("[^A-Za-z0-9 ]", "", names(blank_dominated_features[blank_dominated_features]))
# Data_cleaning_dir
# , ProjectCode,"_",Sys.Date(), 

# Prepare datasets
if(CoRe==T){
  # Split the data into samples and Ctrl samples
  # for fun check
  PCA_SSF_samples <- SSF[media_sample_names,]
  PCA_data_samples <- data[media_sample_names,]
  
  # for fun check
  PCA_SSF_ctrl <- SSF[media_control_names,]
  PCA_data_ctrl <- data[media_control_names,]
  
  PCA_SSF_list <- list(PCA_SSF_samples,PCA_SSF_ctrl)
  PCA_data_list <- list(PCA_data_samples,PCA_data_ctrl)
  

}else{
  PCA_SSF_list <- list(SSF)
  PCA_data_list <- list(data)
}

res <- make_pca_grid(data, SSF, add_to_name = "1. Raw_Raw")
make_signal_drift_reports(data, SSF, add_to_name = "1. Raw_Raw")

if(CoRe==T){
  # Split the data into samples and Ctrl samples
  # for fun check
  SSF_samples <- SSF[media_sample_names,]
  data_samples <-  data[media_sample_names,]
  
  # for fun check
  SSF_ctrl <- SSF[media_control_names,]
  data_ctrl <- data[media_control_names,]
  
  SSF_list <- list(SSF_samples,SSF_ctrl)
  data_list <- list(data_samples,data_ctrl)
}else{
  SSF_list <- list(SSF)
  data_list <- list(data)
}
paste('### ---- 1. Data Cleaning - #1.0 Preparation Finished!')
```

#1.1. Incosistent Features for TF peak verification.
```{r}
# Start new col in metabolite metadata
Metabolite_Metadata$Inconsistent_peaks <- FALSE

# Loop over datasets
for(i in 1:length(SSF_list)){ #i=1
  
  # Dataset selection
  SSF = SSF_list[[i]]
  data = data_list[[i]]
  if(i==1){
    outname<- "sample_"
  }else{
    outname<- "ctrl_"
  }
  
  # Remove blanks from data
  unconcistentTFpeak_data <- data[!rownames(data) %in% rownames(SSF)[tolower(SSF$Conditions) == blank_samp_id],]
  unconcistentTFpeak_SSF <-  SSF[!rownames(SSF) %in% rownames(SSF)[tolower(SSF$Conditions) == blank_samp_id],]
  # Find columns with zeros
  cols_with_zeros <- colnames(unconcistentTFpeak_data)[colSums(unconcistentTFpeak_data == 0, na.rm = TRUE) > 0]
  
  # Get columns with at least one zero and subset the data
  unconcistentTFpeak_data_with_zeros <- unconcistentTFpeak_data[, cols_with_zeros]
  
  # Initialize result list
  result <- list()
  all_values_mixgroups <- vector()
  
  # Loop over metabolites with at least one zero
  for (col in colnames(unconcistentTFpeak_data_with_zeros)) {    # col = colnames(unconcistentTFpeak_data_with_zeros)[1]
    metabolite_values <- unconcistentTFpeak_data_with_zeros[[col]]
    groups <- unconcistentTFpeak_SSF$Conditions
    unique_groups <- unique(groups)
    group_results <- setNames(rep(FALSE, length(unique_groups)), unique_groups)
    
    for (grp in unique_groups) {   # grp = unique_groups[2]
      group_values <- metabolite_values[groups == grp]
      has_zero <- any(group_values == 0, na.rm = TRUE)
      has_value <- any(group_values != 0, na.rm = TRUE)
      group_results[grp] <- has_zero & has_value
      if (has_zero & has_value) {
        all_values_mixgroups <- c(all_values_mixgroups, group_values)
      }
    }
    result[[col]] <- group_results
  }
  
  # Build result table
  result_df <- do.call(rbind, result)
  result_df_filtered <- result_df[apply(result_df, 1, any), ]  # Keep features with at least one TRUE
  result_df_filtered <- as.data.frame(t(result_df_filtered))
  result_df_filtered <- rownames_to_column(result_df_filtered, "SampleGroup")
  
  # Get corresponding values
  result_df_filtered_values <- unconcistentTFpeak_data[, colnames(unconcistentTFpeak_data) %in% colnames(result_df_filtered)]
  result_df_filtered_values <- rownames_to_column(result_df_filtered_values, "SampleGroup")
  result_df_filtered_values$Sample <- result_df_filtered_values$SampleGroup
  result_df_filtered_values$SampleGroup <- unconcistentTFpeak_SSF[result_df_filtered_values$SampleGroup, "Conditions"]
  result_df_filtered_values <- result_df_filtered_values %>% relocate(Sample, .before = everything())
  
  # add cross to the names in the original data, Example: add † to each column name
  Incosistent_metab_names <- colnames(data)[colnames(data) %in% colnames(result_df_filtered)]
  colnames(data_raw)[gsub("\u2020", "",colnames(data_raw)) %in% colnames(result_df_filtered)] <- paste0(Incosistent_metab_names, "\u2020")  
  # "\u2020" = †
  
  # Excel export
  wb <- createWorkbook()
  
  # Sheet 1: Boolean matrix
  addWorksheet(wb, "boolean")
  writeData(wb, "boolean", result_df_filtered)
  conditionalFormatting(wb, "boolean",
                        cols = 2:ncol(result_df_filtered),
                        rows = 2:(nrow(result_df_filtered) + 1),
                        type = "contains",
                        rule = "TRUE",
                        style = createStyle(bgFill = "#FFC7CE")
  )
  
  # Sheet 2: Raw intensity values
  addWorksheet(wb, "values")
  writeData(wb, "values", result_df_filtered_values)
  
  # Style for highlighting
  redStyle <- createStyle(fgFill = "#FFC7CE", fontColour = "#9C0006")
  
  # Highlight cells where inconsistency was detected
  for (r in 1:nrow(result_df_filtered)) {
    sample_group <- result_df_filtered[r, "SampleGroup"]
    for (c in colnames(result_df_filtered)[-1]) {
      if (isTRUE(result_df_filtered[r, c])) {
        matching_rows <- which(result_df_filtered_values$SampleGroup == sample_group)
        matching_col <- which(colnames(result_df_filtered_values) == c)
        for (row in matching_rows) {
          addStyle(wb, "values", style = redStyle, rows = row + 1, cols = matching_col, stack = TRUE)
        }
      }
    }
  }
  
  # Save the Excel file
  saveWorkbook(wb, file = file.path(Data_cleaning_dir, paste0("Incosistent_Features_for_TF_peak_verification_",outname ,ProjectCode,"_",Sys.Date(), ".xlsx")), overwrite = TRUE)
  
  
  
  
  ### ----- Make data values distribution plots with highlighted the values of Inconsistent peaks. ---- ###
  
  # Flatten the data into one long vector
  all_values <- as.vector(as.matrix(unconcistentTFpeak_data))
  
  # Create a dataframe marking whether each value is in your vector
  plot_df <- data.frame(
    Value = all_values,
    Highlight = ifelse(all_values %in% all_values_mixgroups, "Highlighted", "Background"))
  
  # Plot with x-axis limited from 0 to 100,000
  mixedGroupDist <- ggplot(plot_df, aes(x = Value, fill = Highlight)) +
    geom_histogram(bins = 300000, alpha = 0.7, position = "identity") +
    scale_fill_manual(values = c("Background" = "grey70", "Highlighted" = "red")) +
    coord_cartesian(xlim = c(0, 20000000)) +
    labs(title = "Distribution of All Values with Highlighted values of Incosistent Features",
         x = "Value",
         y = "Count") +
    theme_minimal()
  
  # Save plot
  ggsave(filename = file.path(Data_cleaning_dir, paste0("zeroMixedGroups_Distribution_",outname , ProjectCode,"_",Sys.Date(), ".svg")), 
         plot = mixedGroupDist, width = 14, height = 8)
  
  # Update Metabolite metadata
  Metabolite_Metadata$Inconsistent_peaks <-  Metabolite_Metadata$Inconsistent_peaks |Metabolite_Metadata$Metabolite %in% Incosistent_metab_names
  
  write.xlsx(Metabolite_Metadata, file.path(Data_cleaning_dir,"Metabolite_Metadata.xlsx"), colNames = TRUE)
}
paste0("#1.1. Incosistent Features for TF peak verification Finished!")
```

#1.2. Remove metabolites in blanks.
```{r}
Metabolite_Metadata$`SNratio<3`  <- FALSE
for(i in 1:length(SSF_list)){ #i=1
  
  SSF = SSF_list[[i]]
  data = data_list[[i]]
  PCA_SSF = PCA_SSF_list[[i]]
  PCA_data = PCA_data_list[[i]]
  if(i==1){
    outname<- "sample_"
  }else{
    outname<- "ctrl_"
  }
  
  # Split data into blanks and real samples
  blank_rows <- rownames(SSF)[grep("Blank", SSF$Conditions)]
  sample_rows <- rownames(SSF)[!rownames(SSF) %in% blank_rows]
  
  # Subset data
  data_blanks <- data[blank_rows, , drop = FALSE]
  data_samples <- data[sample_rows, , drop = FALSE]
  
  # Calculate feature means across blanks and samples
  blank_means <- colMeans(data_blanks, na.rm = TRUE)
  blank_means[is.na(blank_means)] <- 0
  
  sample_means <- colMeans(data_samples, na.rm = TRUE)
  sample_means[is.na(sample_means)] <- 0
  
  # Set Signal to Noise ratio threshold (e.g., sample mean must be >3x blank mean)
  SignaltoNoiseRatio_threshold <- SignaltoNoiseRatio_threshold
  keep_features <- sample_means > (SignaltoNoiseRatio_threshold * blank_means)
  
  # Keep only features passing the filter
  filtered_data <- data_samples[, keep_features, drop = FALSE]
  
  # For features not passing the filter, set their values in samples to zero
  blank_dominated_features <- !keep_features
  blank_dominated_data <- data[, blank_dominated_features, drop = FALSE]
  
  # Make barplot of Blank dominated metabolites and save it for visual inspection.
  blank_drift <- list()
  for (metab in colnames(blank_dominated_data)) {   # metab = colnames(data)[1]
    # Merge metabolite values with metadata
    plotdata <- merge(blank_dominated_data %>% select(all_of(metab)), SSF, by = 0)
    
    # Ensure RunOrder is numeric and sort
    plotdata <- plotdata[order(plotdata$RunOrder), ]
    
    # Make Row.names a factor ordered by RunOrder
    plotdata$Row.names <- factor(plotdata$Row.names, levels = plotdata$Row.names)
    
    # Plot with Colors column used for custom colors but Conditions for legend
    p <- ggplot(plotdata, aes(x = Row.names, y = .data[[metab]], fill = Conditions)) +
      geom_col(position = "dodge") +
      scale_fill_manual(values = setNames(plotdata$Color, plotdata$Conditions)) + # map colors
      labs(title = paste(metab),
           x = "Samples (Run Order)", y = "Intensity") +
      theme_minimal() +
      theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1, size = 6),  # rotate x labels
            plot.title = element_text(size = 14))
    
    # Store plot
    blank_drift[[metab]] <- p
  }
  
  # Save blank Bar-plots
  BlankPlotsloc <- file.path(Data_cleaning_dir,paste0("BlankBarPlots_" ,outname , ProjectCode,"_",Sys.Date(), ".pdf"))
  pdf(BlankPlotsloc, width = 8, height = 8) # Adjust width and height as needed
  
  # Iterate over the list of plots and print each one
  for (plot in blank_drift) {
    print(plot)
  }
  
  # Close the PDF device
  dev.off()
  
  # # remove
  # data <- filtered_data
  # SSF <- SSF[rownames(SSF) %in% rownames(data),]
  
  # Update metabolite metadata
  Metabolite_Metadata$`SNratio<3` <-  Metabolite_Metadata$`SNratio<3` |Metabolite_Metadata$Metabolite %in% gsub("\u2020", "", names(blank_dominated_features[blank_dominated_features]))
  
  write.xlsx(Metabolite_Metadata, file.path(Data_cleaning_dir,"Metabolite_Metadata.xlsx"), colNames = TRUE)
  
  # Remove blanks
  
  SSF <- SSF[-grep(blank_samp_id , rownames(SSF)),]
  data <- data[-grep(blank_samp_id , rownames(data)),]  
  # Remove blanks from data
  SSF_list[[i]] <- SSF
  data_list[[i]] <- data
  
  
  # PCA
  # # remove
  PCA_data <- filtered_data
  PCA_SSF <- SSF[rownames(SSF) %in% rownames(PCA_data),]
  make_pca_grid(PCA_data, PCA_SSF, add_to_name = paste0("2. ",outname,"Raw_Blank_removed"))
  make_signal_drift_reports(PCA_data, PCA_SSF, add_to_name =  paste0("2. ",outname,"Raw_Blank_removed"))
  PCA_SSF_list[[i]] <- PCA_SSF
  PCA_data_list[[i]] <- PCA_data
}
paste0("#1.2. Remove metabolites in blanks Finished!")
```


#1.3. Feature filtering for missing values in samples and pools. 80% modified rule.
```{r}
#Here we use the 80% modified rule so we allow for ~20% missing values. We look at the smallest sample group. Fοr the pools we do the same but we always have to have at least 4 Pool samples.

Metabolite_Metadata$FeatureFiltering  <- FALSE
for(i in 1:length(SSF_list)){ #i=1
  
  SSF = SSF_list[[i]]
  data = data_list[[i]]
  PCA_SSF = PCA_SSF_list[[i]]
  PCA_data = PCA_data_list[[i]]
  if(i==1){
    outname<- "sample_"
  }else{
    outname<- "ctrl_"
  }
  
  # remake the zeros into NAs 
  data[data==0] <- NA
  # Parameters
  Feature_Filt_Value <- Feature_Filt_Value # Set sample filtering value
  Feature_Filt_Value_Pools <- Feature_Filt_Value_Pools # Set pool filtering value \
  # If we have 5 samples/pools, we allow 1 0/NA in 5 which means 75 not 80
  # If we have 4 samples/pools, we allow 1 0/NA in 4 which means 75 not 80
  
  # Get features to filter for each group
  filter_results <- list()
  
  ## Here I am excluding pool ###
  for (group in setdiff(unique(SSF$Conditions), c("Blank")) ) { # group = setdiff(unique(SSF$Conditions), "Blank")[1]
    
    threshold <- if (isTRUE(group == SSF$Conditions[grep("pool", tolower(SSF$Conditions))[1]]))
  Feature_Filt_Value_Pools else Feature_Filt_Value
    
    # Subset data and conditions
    idx <- which(SSF$Conditions == group)
    sub_data <- data[idx, , drop = FALSE]
    sub_conditions <- SSF$Conditions[idx]
    
    # Skip if no data
    if (length(idx) == 0 || length(unique(sub_conditions)) < 1) {
      filter_results[[group]] <- character(0)
      next
    }
    
    # Split by condition
    split_data <- split(sub_data, sub_conditions)
    #split_data <- split(data, SSF$Conditions)
    
    feature_missing <- c() 
    
    
    for (df in split_data) { # df = split_data[1]
      df <- as.data.frame(df)
      for (col in seq_len(ncol(df))) {    #   col=18
        if (sum(is.na(df[, col])) > round((1 - threshold) * nrow(df)) ) { # 0.8 means, we allow up but NOT equal to 20 NA vales.
          # We Use greater than the threshold. So, 1 NA in 5 non NA is allowed.
          feature_missing <- c(feature_missing, col)
        }
      }
    }
    filter_results[[group]] <-  colnames(data)[unique(feature_missing)]
  }
  
  # Remove a feature if it is not found in the pools
  to_filter <- c()
  to_filter <- filter_results[[ as.character(SSF$Conditions[ grep("pool", tolower(SSF$Conditions))[1] ]) ]]
  
  # Keep only features missing in *all* conditions ignoring pools
  feature_missing <- unlist(filter_results)
  if (length(feature_missing) > 0) {
    count <- table(feature_missing)
    for (c in names(count)) { 
      if (count[[c]] == length(unique(SSF$Conditions[-grep("pool", tolower(SSF$Conditions))])) ) {
        to_filter <- c(to_filter, c)
      }
    }
  }
  
  # Combine
  filtered_features <- unique(to_filter)
  
  # Filter data now. Otherwise they are removed when its time for them to be removed. 
  #data <- data[, !colnames(data) %in% filtered_features, drop = FALSE]
  
  features_filtered_data <- data[,filtered_features]
  features_filtered_data <- rownames_to_column(features_filtered_data, "Samples")
  
  # Save values for visual inspection
  write.xlsx(features_filtered_data, file.path(Data_cleaning_dir,"Features_FIltered_data.csv"), colNames = TRUE)
  
  # Update metabolite metadata
  Metabolite_Metadata$FeatureFiltering <-  Metabolite_Metadata$FeatureFiltering |Metabolite_Metadata$Metabolite %in% gsub("\u2020", "",filtered_features)
```
