```{r}
  write.xlsx(Metabolite_Metadata, file.path(Data_cleaning_dir,"Metabolite_Metadata.xlsx"), colNames = TRUE)
  
  # PCA
  # # remove
  PCA_data <- PCA_data <- PCA_data[, !colnames(PCA_data) %in% filtered_features, drop = FALSE]
  PCA_SSF <- SSF[rownames(SSF) %in% rownames(PCA_data),]
  make_pca_grid(PCA_data, PCA_SSF, add_to_name =  paste0("3. ",outname,"NA_filtered"))
  make_signal_drift_reports(PCA_data, PCA_SSF, add_to_name = paste0("3. ",outname,"NA_filtered"))
  PCA_SSF_list[[i]] <- PCA_SSF
  PCA_data_list[[i]] <- PCA_data
}
paste0("#1.3. Feature filtering for missing values in samples and pools. 80% modified rule Finished!")
```

#1.4. CV filtering. 30% filtering.
```{r}
Threshold_cv = Threshold_cv

for(i in 1:length(SSF_list)){ # i=1
  
  SSF = SSF_list[[i]]
  data = data_list[[i]]
  PCA_SSF = PCA_SSF_list[[i]]
  PCA_data = PCA_data_list[[i]]
  if(i==1){
    outname<- "sample_"
  }else{
    outname<- "ctrl_"
  }
  
   if(CoRe == T & i==1 ){
   ## -------- Pool Coefficient of variation Estimation ------ ##
  #A. Pool
  pool_data <- data[rownames(SSF)[SSF$Conditions == SSF$Conditions[grep("pool", tolower(SSF$Conditions))[1]]], ]
  # Calculate the Coefficient of Variation
  result_df <- apply(pool_data, 2,  function(x) { (sd(x, na.rm =T)/  mean(x, na.rm =T))*100 }  ) %>% t()%>% as.data.frame()
  rownames(result_df)[1] <- "Pool_CV"
  # reorder 
  result_df <- result_df[, names(pool_data)]
  # calculate the percentage of  NAs
  NAvector <- apply(pool_data, 2,  function(x) { (sum(is.na(x))/length(x))*100 }  )#%>% t()%>% as.data.frame()
  # Make the final dataframe
  result_df_final <- result_df %>% t() %>% as.data.frame()%>% mutate(HighCV = Pool_CV > Threshold_cv)# %>% as.data.frame()
  result_df_final$Pool_MissingValuePercentage <- NAvector
  rownames(result_df_final)<- colnames(pool_data)
  
  #b. Sample
  sample_data <- data[rownames(SSF)[SSF$Conditions != SSF$Conditions[grep("pool", tolower(SSF$Conditions))[1]]], ]
  # Calculate the Coefficient of Variation
  result_df <- apply(sample_data, 2,  function(x) { (sd(x, na.rm =T)/  mean(x, na.rm =T))*100 }  ) %>% t()%>% as.data.frame()
  rownames(result_df)[1] <- "Sample_CV"
  # reorder 
  result_df <- result_df[, names(sample_data)]
  # calculate the percentage of  NAs
  NAvector <- apply(sample_data, 2,  function(x) { (sum(is.na(x))/length(x))*100 }  )#%>% t()%>% as.data.frame()
  # Make the final dataframe
  result_df_final <- merge(result_df_final, result_df %>% t() %>% as.data.frame(), by=0)
  colnames(result_df_final)[1] <- "Metabolite" 
  result_df_final <- result_df_final[match(colnames(data), result_df_final$Metabolite), ]
  
  #### -------------------------- CV histogram --------------------- ####
  # Reshape to long format
  long_df <- result_df_final %>%
    select(Metabolite, Pool_CV, Sample_CV) %>%
    pivot_longer(cols = c(Pool_CV, Sample_CV), names_to = "Type", values_to = "CV") %>%
    mutate(Type = ifelse(Type == "Pool_CV", "Pool", "Sample"))
  
  # Create plot with stronger color distinction
  HistCVcombined <- ggplot(long_df, aes(x = CV, fill = Type, color = Type)) +
    geom_density(alpha = 0.3, size = 1.0) +
    geom_vline(aes(xintercept = Threshold_cv), color = "black", linetype = "dashed", size = 1) +
    labs(title = "Coefficient of Variation for Pool and Sample",
         x = "Coefficient of Variation (CV%)",
         y = "Density",
         caption = paste0(nrow(result_df_final), " total features")) +
    scale_fill_manual(values = c("Pool" = "red", "Sample" = "blue")) +
    scale_color_manual(values = c("Pool" = "red", "Sample" = "blue")) +
    theme_classic()
  
  # Add Valine-d8 (IS) line if present
  if (length(grep(valine_pattern_id, tolower(result_df_final$Metabolite))) > 0) {
    val_cv_pool <- result_df_final$Pool_CV[grep(valine_pattern_id, tolower(result_df_final$Metabolite))]
    val_cv_samp <- result_df_final$Sample_CV[grep(valine_pattern_id, tolower(result_df_final$Metabolite))]
    
    HistCVcombined <- HistCVcombined +
      geom_vline(xintercept = val_cv_pool, color = "red", linetype = "dashed", size = 1) +
      geom_vline(xintercept = val_cv_samp, color = "blue", linetype = "dashed", size = 1) +
      scale_x_continuous(breaks = c(30, 50, 100, val_cv_pool),
                         labels = c("30", "50", "100", "Valine-d8 (IS)"))
  }
  
  # Save
  ggsave(filename = file.path(Data_cleaning_dir,paste0(outname ,"CV_Histogram.svg")), 
         plot = HistCVcombined, width = 12, height = 6)
  
  message("n of metabolites removed because of RSD > " ,Threshold_cv, "% : " , sum(result_df_final$HighCV==T, na.rm = T))
  
  # Remove high cv features
  # sample_data_high_cv_feats <- colnames(data)[result_df_final$HighCV==T]
  # data <- data[,!colnames(data) %in% sample_data_high_cv_feats]
  
  # Save resulting table
  result_df_final$Metabolite <- gsub("\u2020", "",result_df_final$Metabolite)
  
  colnames(result_df_final)[colnames(result_df_final) %in% c("HighCV", "Pool_CV", "Pool_MissingValuePercentage", "Sample_CV")] <- 
    paste0(outname, c("HighCV", "Pool_CV","Pool_MissingValuePercentage" ,"Sample_CV"))
  
  Metabolite_Metadata <- merge(Metabolite_Metadata, result_df_final, "Metabolite")
  write.xlsx(Metabolite_Metadata, file.path(Data_cleaning_dir,"Metabolite_Metadata.xlsx"), colNames = TRUE)
   }
  
     if(CoRe == T & i==2 ){
       Metabolite_Metadata <- read.xlsx( file.path(Data_cleaning_dir, "Metabolite_Metadata.xlsx"))
     }
 
  
  # PCA
  # # remove
  sample_data_high_cv_feats <- colnames(data)[Metabolite_Metadata$sample_Pool_CV ==T]
  PCA_data <- PCA_data[,!colnames(PCA_data) %in% sample_data_high_cv_feats]
  PCA_SSF <- SSF[rownames(SSF) %in% rownames(PCA_data),]
  make_pca_grid(PCA_data, PCA_SSF, add_to_name = paste0("4. ",outname,"CV_filtered"))
  make_signal_drift_reports(PCA_data, PCA_SSF, add_to_name =  paste0("4. ",outname,"CV_filtered"))
  PCA_SSF_list[[i]] <- PCA_SSF
  PCA_data_list[[i]] <- PCA_data
}

paste0("#1.4. CV filtering. 30% filtering Finished!")
```

#1.5. Single Outlier Values filtering. Skip generally
```{r eval = FALSE}
data$SampleID <- rownames(data)
SSF$SampleID <- rownames(SSF)

long_data <- data %>%
  pivot_longer(-SampleID, names_to = "Metabolite", values_to = "Value") %>%
  left_join(SSF[, c("SampleID", "Conditions")], by = "SampleID")

# Log data to make them ~ normally distributed
#long_data$Value <- log2(long_data$Value)
# Placeholder to collect outliers
outlier_results <- list()

for(selected_metabolite in  unique(long_data$Metabolite)){ # selected_metabolite = unique(long_data$Metabolite)[1]
  if (selected_metabolite == "Valine-d8") next
  # Filter for that metabolite
  metab_data <- long_data %>% filter(Metabolite == selected_metabolite)
  
  # Loop over conditions
  for (cond in unique(metab_data$Conditions)) { # cond = unique(metab_data$Conditions)[1]
    group_data <- metab_data %>% filter(Conditions == cond)
    
    if(sum(!is.na(group_data$Value))>1){
      for (i in seq_len(nrow(group_data))) {  #   i =  seq_len(nrow(group_data))[1]
        test_val <- group_data$Value[i]
        test_sample <- group_data$SampleID[i]
        
        # Leave-one-out: remove current value
        rest <- group_data$Value[-i]
        
        if(length(rest[!is.na(rest)])>1){
          m <- mean(rest, na.rm = TRUE)
          s <- sd(rest, na.rm = TRUE)
          
          lower <- m - 4 * s
          upper <- m + 4 * s
          
          if (!is.na(test_val) && (test_val < lower || test_val > upper)) {
            outlier_results[[length(outlier_results) + 1]] <- data.frame(
              SampleID = test_sample,
              Condition = cond,
              Value = test_val,
              Mean_rest = m,
              SD_rest = s,
              Metabolite = selected_metabolite
            )
          }  
        }
      }
    }
  }
}

# Combine and print
if (length(outlier_results) > 0) {
  # Make outliers values into a df
  outlier_results <- outlier_results[!sapply(outlier_results, is.null)]
  # Combine all into a single data.frame
  outlier_df <- do.call(rbind, outlier_results)
  # View result
  head(outlier_df)
  
  message(nrow(outlier_df)  ," Outlier values found.\n")
} else {
  cat("No outliers found.\n")
}

#remove the sample IDs
data$SampleID <- NULL
SSF$SampleID <- NULL

# remove features  skip

# # Loop through each outlier and set it to NA
# for (i in seq_len(nrow(outlier_df))) {  # i =seq_len(nrow(outlier_df))[1]
#   samp <- outlier_df$SampleID[i]
#   feat <- outlier_df$Metabolite[i]
#   data[samp, feat] <- NA
# }

# DO NOT remove single outler. Its not working properly yet. is gives hints but dont remove
# # measure cv. if its high then 
# x <- group_data$Value
# med <- median(x)
# mad_val <- mad(x)  # default is scaled for normal distribution
# 
# # Robust Z-scores
# robust_z <- (x - med) / mad_val
# which(abs(robust_z) > 2.5)
# if its found agaib then remove? or do something about
paste0("#1.5. Single Outlier Values filtering!")
```

Filter features

Here I am filtering only with Feature filtering. In standard pipeline we filter with the filtered column.
```{r}
# Ensure metabolite order from data colnames
met_order <-    gsub("\u2020", "", colnames(data_raw))

# reorder metadata to match that order
Metabolite_Metadata <- Metabolite_Metadata[match(met_order, Metabolite_Metadata$Metabolite), ]

# Metabolite_Metadata <- Metabolite_Metadata %>%
#   mutate(Filtered = if_any(
#     everything(),  # check all other columns
#     ~ .x == TRUE))

Metabolite_Metadata <- Metabolite_Metadata %>%
  mutate(Filtered = if_any(-Inconsistent_peaks, ~ .x == TRUE))

write.xlsx(Metabolite_Metadata, file.path(Data_cleaning_dir,"Metabolite_Metadata.xlsx"), colNames = TRUE)

# filter
metabs_to_remvove <- Metabolite_Metadata %>% filter(Filtered) %>% pull(Metabolite)

## Here we use the data_raw but the background subtracted data data_raw
data <- data_raw[, !gsub("†", "", colnames(data_raw)) %in% metabs_to_remvove, drop = FALSE]

# Remove blanks
blank_samples <- rownames(SSF_raw)[tolower(SSF_raw$Conditions) == "blank"]

data <- data[!rownames(data) %in% blank_samples, ]
SSF <- SSF_raw[!rownames(SSF_raw) %in% blank_samples, ]

paste0("### ---- 1. Data Cleaning ---- Finished!")
```



### ----- 2. Data Normalisation -----
#2.0 Preparation
```{r}
# Set sub folders
Data_normalisation_dir <- file.path(output.dir, "2. Data Normalisation")
if (!dir.exists(Data_normalisation_dir)) dir.create(Data_normalisation_dir, recursive = TRUE)

# Metabolite_Metadata$`SNratio<3` <- rownames(Metabolite_Metadata) %in% gsub("[^A-Za-z0-9 ]", "", names(blank_dominated_features[blank_dominated_features]))
# Data_cleaning_dir
# , ProjectCode,"_",Sys.Date(), 
  
if(CoRe==T){
  # Split the data into samples and Ctrl samples
  # for fun check
  SSF_samples <- SSF[media_sample_names[!grepl(blank_samp_id, media_sample_names)],]
  data_samples <- data[media_sample_names[!grepl(blank_samp_id, media_sample_names)],]
  
  # for fun check
  SSF_ctrl <- SSF[media_control_names[!grepl(blank_samp_id, media_control_names)],]
  data_ctrl <- data[media_control_names[!grepl(blank_samp_id, media_control_names)],]
  
  SSF_list <- list(SSF_samples,SSF_ctrl)
  data_list <- list(data_samples,data_ctrl)
}else{
  SSF_list <- list(SSF)
  data_list <- list(data)
}
paste0("#2.0 Preparation Finished!")
```

#2.1. IS normalisation valine-d8

# Check if TIC is required or VAlineIS normalisation
```{r}
for(i in 1:length(SSF_list)){ # i=1
  
  SSF = SSF_list[[i]]
  data = data_list[[i]]
  PCA_SSF = PCA_SSF_list[[i]]
  PCA_data = PCA_data_list[[i]]
  if(i==1){
    outname<- "sample_"
  }else{
    outname<- "ctrl_"
  }
  
  ### ### Check TIC -----------------
  SSF$TIC <- rowSums(data[rownames(SSF), , drop = FALSE], na.rm = TRUE)
  
  # 2) make a discrete key (because your function uses scale_color_manual)
  SSF$TIC_key <- factor(rank(-SSF$TIC, ties.method = "first"))  # 1 = highest TIC
  
  # 3) map TIC rank to a gradient (high=red, low=blue)
  tic_pal <- colorRampPalette(c("red","blue"))(nrow(SSF))
  SSF$TIC_hex <- tic_pal[as.integer(SSF$TIC_key)]
  
  # 4) feed your function: set Color to hex, color_col to TIC_key
  SSF$colorsave <- SSF$Color
  SSF$Color <- SSF$TIC_hex
  
  make_pca_grid(data, SSF,
                add_to_name = paste0("5.0 " ,outname, "TIC_gradient_CHECK"),
                color_col   = "TIC_key")
  
  # Fix SSF
  SSF$Color <- SSF$colorsave
  SSF$TIC_hex <- NULL
  SSF$TIC_key <- NULL
  SSF$colorsave <- NULL
  SSF$TIC <- NULL
  
  ### ### Check Valine -----------
  # 1) Valine intensity per sample
  SSF$VAL <- data[rownames(SSF), "valine-d8"]
  
  # 2) rank (highest = 1)
  SSF$VAL_key <- factor(rank(-SSF$VAL, ties.method = "first"))
  
  # 3) gradient (high=red, low=blue)
  val_pal <- colorRampPalette(c("red","blue"))(nrow(SSF))
  SSF$VAL_hex <- val_pal[as.integer(SSF$VAL_key)]
  
  # 4) feed PCA function
  SSF$colorsave <- SSF$Color
  SSF$Color <- SSF$VAL_hex
  
  make_pca_grid(data, SSF,
                add_to_name = paste0("5.1 " ,outname, "Valine_gradient_CHECK"),
                color_col   = "VAL_key")
  
  # restore SSF
  SSF$Color <- SSF$colorsave
  SSF$VAL_hex <- NULL
  SSF$VAL_key <- NULL
  SSF$colorsave <- NULL
  SSF$VAL <- NULL
}
```

# TIC normalisation
```{r }
# shoud you norlaise with tic? CHEKC IF YOU SEE THE PATTERN IN PCA

for(i in 1:length(SSF_list)){ # i=1
  
  SSF = SSF_list[[i]]
  data = data_list[[i]]
  PCA_SSF = PCA_SSF_list[[i]]
  PCA_data = PCA_data_list[[i]]
  if(i==1){
    outname<- "sample_"
  }else{
    outname<- "ctrl_"
  }
  
  tic <- rowSums(data_list[[i]], na.rm = TRUE)
  tic_ref <- median(tic, na.rm = TRUE)
  
  data_tic <- sweep(data_list[[i]], 1, tic, "/") * tic_ref
  
  SSF <- SSF_list[[i]]
  # PCA
  make_pca_grid(data_tic, SSF, add_to_name = paste0("5.5 " ,outname, "TIC_normalised"))
  make_signal_drift_reports(data_tic, SSF, add_to_name = paste0("5.5 " ,outname , "TIC_normalised"))
  
  make_pca_grid(data_tic, SSF, add_to_name = paste0("5.5 TIC_normalised_Big"), fig_size_cm = c(120, 80))
  
  data_list[[i]] <-data_tic 
}
paste0("#2.1 TIC normalisation Finished!")
```


#2.2. Signal Drift normalisation using LoessQC

```{r}
library(qcrlscR)

suppressPackageStartupMessages({
  library(qcrlscR)
  library(dplyr)
  library(tibble)
  library(ggplot2)
  library(gridExtra)
  library(openxlsx)
})

# ---------- INPUT ----------
data <- data_list[[1]]
SSF <- SSF_list[[1]]

# ---------- ORDER BY RUN ORDER ----------
ord <- order(SSF$RunOrder)
data_o <- data[ord, , drop = FALSE]
SSF_o  <- SSF[ord, , drop = FALSE]

all_samples <- rownames(SSF_o)
feat_names  <- colnames(data_o)

# QC/sample factor for qcrlscR
cls_qc <- ifelse(SSF_o$Conditions == "Pool", "qc", "sample")
cls_qc <- factor(cls_qc, levels = c("qc", "sample"))
cls_bl <- factor(rep(1, nrow(SSF_o)))  # single batch

# colors (expects SSF has: Conditions + Color)
pal_map <- unique(SSF_o[, c("Conditions", "Color")])
customColors <- setNames(pal_map$Color, pal_map$Conditions)

# QC samples (Pools)
qc_samples <- rownames(SSF_o)[tolower(SSF_o$Conditions) == "pool" | grepl("pool", tolower(SSF_o$Conditions))]
samp_ids   <- setdiff(all_samples, qc_samples)
qc_idx     <- which(all_samples %in% qc_samples)

# group labels (within-group CV) only for NON-QC samples
group_labels <- SSF_o$Conditions
names(group_labels) <- rownames(SSF_o)
group_labels_samp <- droplevels(factor(group_labels[samp_ids]))

# ---------- SAFE HELPERS ----------
cv_safe <- function(x) {
  x <- as.numeric(x)
  x <- x[is.finite(x)]
  if (length(x) < 2) return(NA_real_)
  m <- mean(x, na.rm = TRUE)
  if (!is.finite(m) || m == 0) return(NA_real_)
  100 * sd(x, na.rm = TRUE) / m
}

within_cv_safe <- function(x, groups) {
  x <- as.numeric(x)
  ok <- is.finite(x) & !is.na(groups)
  x <- x[ok]
  g <- droplevels(groups[ok])
  
  if (length(x) < 2) return(NA_real_)
  
  cvs <- tapply(x, g, function(v) {
    v <- v[is.finite(v)]
    if (length(v) < 2) return(NA_real_)
    m <- mean(v, na.rm = TRUE)
    if (!is.finite(m) || m == 0) return(NA_real_)
    100 * sd(v, na.rm = TRUE) / m
  })
  
  if (all(is.na(cvs))) NA_real_ else mean(cvs, na.rm = TRUE)
}

fmt1 <- function(x) ifelse(is.finite(x), sprintf("%.1f", x), "NA")

# Excel writer (3 decimals formatting)
write_table_3dp <- function(df, out_path, sheet = "Sheet1") {
  wb <- createWorkbook()
  addWorksheet(wb, sheet)
  writeData(wb, sheet, df)
  
  numStyle3 <- createStyle(numFmt = "0.000")
  num_cols <- which(vapply(df, is.numeric, logical(1)))
  if (length(num_cols) > 0) {
    addStyle(
      wb, sheet, style = numStyle3,
      rows = 2:(nrow(df) + 1),
      cols = num_cols,
      gridExpand = TRUE, stack = TRUE
    )
  }
  
  setColWidths(wb, sheet, cols = 1:ncol(df), widths = "auto")
  saveWorkbook(wb, out_path, overwrite = TRUE)
}

# ---------- NORMALISE (qcrlscR LOESS) ----------
dat_corr <- qc.rlsc.wrap(
  dat    = data_o,
  cls.qc = cls_qc,
  cls.bl = cls_bl,
  method = "divide",
  intra  = TRUE,
  opti   = TRUE,
  log10  = TRUE,
  outl   = TRUE,
  shift  = FALSE
)
rownames(dat_corr) <- rownames(data_o)

# ---------- OUTPUTS ----------
cv_tbl <- data.frame(
  Feature            = feat_names,
  CV_all_Raw         = NA_real_,
  CV_pool_Raw        = NA_real_,
  CV_all_Corrected   = NA_real_,
  CV_pool_Corrected  = NA_real_,
  Diff_CV_all        = NA_real_,
  Diff_CV_pool       = NA_real_,
  WithinCV_Raw       = NA_real_,
  WithinCV_Corrected = NA_real_,
  Diff_WithinCV      = NA_real_,
  Improved_both      = FALSE,
  check.names = FALSE
)

pdf_out <- file.path(Data_normalisation_dir, "qcrlscR_LOESS_Normalisation.pdf")
pdf(pdf_out, width = 14, height = 5)

# ---------- LOOP FEATURES (CALC + PLOTS HERE) ----------
for (feature in feat_names) {
  
  raw_vec  <- data_o[[feature]];  names(raw_vec)  <- rownames(data_o)
  corr_vec <- dat_corr[[feature]]; names(corr_vec) <- rownames(dat_corr)
  
  # CVs: non-QC and pool
  cv_raw_all   <- cv_safe(raw_vec[samp_ids])
  cv_corr_all  <- cv_safe(corr_vec[samp_ids])
  cv_raw_pool  <- cv_safe(raw_vec[qc_samples])
  cv_corr_pool <- cv_safe(corr_vec[qc_samples])
  
  # within-group CVs on NON-QC samples
  wg_raw  <- within_cv_safe(raw_vec[samp_ids],  group_labels_samp)
  wg_corr <- within_cv_safe(corr_vec[samp_ids], group_labels_samp)
  
  # table
  cv_tbl[cv_tbl$Feature == feature,
         c("CV_all_Raw","CV_pool_Raw","CV_all_Corrected","CV_pool_Corrected")] <-
    c(cv_raw_all, cv_raw_pool, cv_corr_all, cv_corr_pool)
  
  cv_tbl[cv_tbl$Feature == feature, c("Diff_CV_all","Diff_CV_pool")] <-
    c(cv_corr_all - cv_raw_all, cv_corr_pool - cv_raw_pool)
  
  cv_tbl[cv_tbl$Feature == feature,
         c("WithinCV_Raw","WithinCV_Corrected","Diff_WithinCV")] <-
    c(wg_raw, wg_corr, wg_corr - wg_raw)
  
  cv_tbl[cv_tbl$Feature == feature, "Improved_both"] <-
    isTRUE(is.finite(cv_raw_all) && is.finite(cv_corr_all) && is.finite(wg_raw) && is.finite(wg_corr) &&
             cv_corr_all < cv_raw_all && wg_corr < wg_raw)
  
  # ---- FIT LINE SHAPE (consistent with QC-RLSC) ----
  tmp_raw <- data_o[, feature, drop = FALSE]
  colnames(tmp_raw) <- "feat"
  
  corr1 <- qc.rlsc(
    x = tmp_raw,
    y = cls_qc,
    method = "divide",
    opti = TRUE
  )
  
  fit_shape <- as.numeric(tmp_raw[, 1] / corr1[, 1])
  fit_shape[!is.finite(fit_shape)] <- NA_real_
  
  # scale to raw intensity scale
  k <- median(tmp_raw[qc_idx, 1], na.rm = TRUE) / median(fit_shape[qc_idx], na.rm = TRUE)
  fit_all_raw  <- fit_shape * k
  fit_all_corr <- rep(median(corr_vec[qc_idx], na.rm = TRUE), nrow(SSF_o))
  
  df_plot <- tibble(
    Sample    = factor(rownames(SSF_o), levels = rownames(SSF_o)),
    Group     = factor(SSF_o$Conditions, levels = unique(SSF_o$Conditions)),
    Raw       = raw_vec,
    Corrected = corr_vec,
    FitRaw    = fit_all_raw,
    FitCorr   = fit_all_corr
  )
  
  # subtitles: left=RAW, right=CORRECTED (include pool CV)
  sub_raw  <- sprintf("Raw: CV(sample)=%s%% | WithinCV=%s%% | CV(pool)=%s%%",
                      fmt1(cv_raw_all), fmt1(wg_raw), fmt1(cv_raw_pool))
  sub_corr <- sprintf("Corrected: CV(sample)=%s%% | WithinCV=%s%% | CV(pool)=%s%%",
                      fmt1(cv_corr_all), fmt1(wg_corr), fmt1(cv_corr_pool))
  
  p_raw <- ggplot(df_plot, aes(x = Sample, y = Raw, color = Group)) +
    geom_point(size = 2) +
    geom_line(aes(y = FitRaw, group = 1), color = "red", linewidth = 0.8) +
    scale_color_manual(values = customColors) +
    scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, NA)) +
    labs(title = paste0(feature, " — Raw"), subtitle = sub_raw) +
    theme_minimal() +
    theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1))
  
  p_corr <- ggplot(df_plot, aes(x = Sample, y = Corrected, color = Group)) +
    geom_point(size = 2) +
    geom_line(aes(y = FitCorr, group = 1), color = "red", linewidth = 0.8) +
    scale_color_manual(values = customColors) +
    scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, NA)) +
    labs(title = paste0(feature, " — Corrected"), subtitle = sub_corr) +
    theme_minimal() +
    theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1))
  
  gridExtra::grid.arrange(p_raw, p_corr, ncol = 2)
}

dev.off()

# ---------- EXPORT EXCEL (per-feature table) ----------
cv_outfile <- file.path(Data_normalisation_dir, "qcrlscR_LOESS_CV_summary.xlsx")
write_table_3dp(cv_tbl, cv_outfile)

# ---------- SUMMARY TABLE (like your three_col_tbl) ----------
# mean differences vs raw (more negative = better)
cv_mean_diff_all    <- mean(cv_tbl$Diff_CV_all,    na.rm = TRUE)
cv_mean_diff_within <- mean(cv_tbl$Diff_WithinCV,  na.rm = TRUE)
cv_mean_diff_pool   <- mean(cv_tbl$Diff_CV_pool,   na.rm = TRUE)

three_col_tbl <- data.frame(
  Method         = "qcrlscR_LOESS",
  CV             = round(cv_mean_diff_all, 4),
  Within_CV      = round(cv_mean_diff_within, 4),
  Pool_CV        = round(cv_mean_diff_pool, 4),
  Improved       = isTRUE(cv_mean_diff_all < 0 & cv_mean_diff_within < 0 & cv_mean_diff_pool < 0),
  check.names = FALSE
)

message("Mean difference vs Raw (more negative = better):")
print(three_col_tbl)

summary_outfile <- file.path(Data_normalisation_dir, "qcrlscR_LOESS_MeanDiff_fromRaw_CVtable.xlsx")
write_table_3dp(three_col_tbl, summary_outfile)

# ---------- corrected matrix (optional) ----------
corr_outfile <- file.path(Data_normalisation_dir, "qcrlscR_LOESS_corrected_matrix_3dp.xlsx")
write_table_3dp(
  df = as.data.frame(round(dat_corr, 3)) %>% tibble::rownames_to_column("Sample"),
  out_path = corr_outfile
)

message("Saved PDF: ", pdf_out)
message("Saved CV table: ", cv_outfile)
message("Saved summary table: ", summary_outfile)
message("Saved corrected matrix (3dp): ", corr_outfile)

# bring back order
data_list[[1]] <- dat_corr[rownames(SSF),]
```

    
#2.3. Missing value imputation
```{r} 
#### Make preMVI boxplots ---------------
Metab_AllValuePLots <- list()
for (metab in colnames(rbind(data_list[[1]], data_list[[2]]))) {   # metab = colnames(data)[1]
  # Merge metabolite values with metadata
  plotdata <- merge(rbind(data_list[[1]], data_list[[2]]) %>% select(all_of(metab)),  rbind(SSF_list[[1]], SSF_list[[2]]), by = 0)
  
  # Ensure RunOrder is numeric and sort
 # plotdata <- plotdata[order(plotdata$RunOrder), ]
  
  # Make Row.names a factor ordered by RunOrder
  plotdata$Row.names <- factor(plotdata$Row.names, levels = plotdata$Row.names)
  
  # Plot with Colors column used for custom colors but Conditions for legend
  p <- ggplot(plotdata, aes(x = Row.names, y = .data[[metab]], fill = Conditions)) +
    geom_col(position = "dodge") +
    scale_fill_manual(values = setNames(plotdata$Color, plotdata$Conditions)) + # map colors
    labs(title = paste(metab),
         x = "Samples (Run Order)", y = "Intensity") +
    theme_minimal() +
    theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1, size = 6),  # rotate x labels
          plot.title = element_text(size = 14))
  
  # Store plot
  Metab_AllValuePLots[[metab]] <- p
}

# Save blank Bar-plots
AllValueBoxplotPreMVIloc <- file.path(output.dir,paste0("Metabolite_AllValues_preMVI_boxplots.pdf"))
pdf(AllValueBoxplotPreMVIloc, width = 14, height = 8) # Adjust width and height as needed

# Iterate over the list of plots and print each one
for (plot in Metab_AllValuePLots) {
  print(plot)
}

# Close the PDF device
dev.off()
    

### ----------------- Missign Value Imputation ------------------
for(i in 1:length(SSF_list)){ #i=1
  # Dataset selection
  SSF = SSF_list[[i]]
  data = data_list[[i]]
  if(i==1){
    outname<- "sample_"
  }else{
    outname<- "ctrl_"
  }
  
  MVI_data <- data 
  SSF <- SSF[match(rownames(MVI_data), rownames(SSF)), ]
  halfmin <- min(MVI_data[MVI_data>0], na.rm = T)/2
  # Because of the loess correction we have zeros and negative values along with the NAs we set them all to zero.
  # MVI_data[MVI_data <halfmin] <- 0
  # MVI_data[is.na(MVI_data)] <- 0
  
  for (feature  in colnames(MVI_data)){ # feature =  colnames(MVI_data)[3]
    feature_data <- merge(MVI_data[feature] , SSF %>% select("Conditions"), by= 0, sort = FALSE)
    feature_data <- column_to_rownames(feature_data, "Row.names")
    feature_data[feature_data==0] <- NA
    
    split_Input <- split(feature_data, SSF[,"Conditions"]) # split data frame into a list of data frames by condition
    
    for (m in split_Input){ # Select dataframe  # m <- split_Input[[2]]
      if( sum(is.na(m[,1]))== (length(m[,1])) |  sum(is.na(m[,1]))== (length(m[,1])-1) ){ #  if  all is NA or we have only 1 value then put 0 in whole group 
        set.seed(1234)
        for (value in  1:length( MVI_data[rownames(MVI_data) %in% rownames(m),feature])){ # value = 1
          MVI_data[rownames(MVI_data) %in% rownames(m),feature][value] <- halfmin + sample(100:1000, 1, replace = TRUE)
        }
      }else{ # if we have more than 1 value
        MVI_data[rownames(MVI_data) %in% rownames(m),feature][is.na(m[,1])]<- mean(m[,1],na.rm = TRUE )
      }
    }
  }   
  
  # set.seed(123) # Set seed for reproducibility
  # MVI_data[MVI_data == 0] <- 1 + runif(sum(MVI_data == 0), min = 0.01, max = 0.1)
  
  # PCA
  make_pca_grid(MVI_data, SSF, add_to_name =  paste0("7. ",outname,"Imputed"))
  make_signal_drift_reports(MVI_data, SSF, add_to_name =  paste0("7. ",outname,"Imputed"))
  
  data_list[[i]] <- MVI_data
}



###  Make postMVI boxplots -------------------
Metab_AllValuePLots <- list()
for (metab in colnames(rbind(data_list[[1]], data_list[[2]]))) {   # metab = colnames(data)[1]
  # Merge metabolite values with metadata
  plotdata <- merge(rbind(data_list[[1]], data_list[[2]]) %>% select(all_of(metab)),  rbind(SSF_list[[1]], SSF_list[[2]]), by = 0)
  
  # Ensure RunOrder is numeric and sort
 # plotdata <- plotdata[order(plotdata$RunOrder), ]
  
  # Make Row.names a factor ordered by RunOrder
  plotdata$Row.names <- factor(plotdata$Row.names, levels = plotdata$Row.names)
  
  # Plot with Colors column used for custom colors but Conditions for legend
  p <- ggplot(plotdata, aes(x = Row.names, y = .data[[metab]], fill = Conditions)) +
    geom_col(position = "dodge") +
    scale_fill_manual(values = setNames(plotdata$Color, plotdata$Conditions)) + # map colors
    labs(title = paste(metab),
         x = "Samples (Run Order)", y = "Intensity") +
    theme_minimal() +
    theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1, size = 6),  # rotate x labels
          plot.title = element_text(size = 14))
  
  # Store plot
  Metab_AllValuePLots[[metab]] <- p
}

# Save blank Bar-plots
AllValueBoxplotPreMVIloc <- file.path(output.dir,paste0("Metabolite_AllValues_postMVI_boxplots.pdf"))
pdf(AllValueBoxplotPreMVIloc, width = 14, height = 8) # Adjust width and height as needed

# Iterate over the list of plots and print each one
for (plot in Metab_AllValuePLots) {
  print(plot)
}

# Close the PDF device
dev.off()

paste("#2.3. Missing value imputation Finished!")
```


# CoRe normalisation using media blanks
```{r}
# -------------------- SETUP --------------------
norm_dir <- file.path(Data_normalisation_dir)
if (!dir.exists(norm_dir)) dir.create(norm_dir, recursive = TRUE)

if (CoRe == TRUE) {
  

  # -------------------- INPUT --------------------
  media_blank_data <- data_list[[2]]
  media_blank_ssf  <- SSF_list[[2]]
  
  sample_data <- data_list[[1]]
  sample_ssf  <- SSF_list[[1]]
  
  # Keep SSF aligned to sample_data rows
  common_samples <- intersect(rownames(sample_data), rownames(sample_ssf))
  sample_data <- sample_data[common_samples, , drop = FALSE]
  sample_ssf  <- sample_ssf[common_samples, , drop = FALSE]
  
  # -------------------- BLANK MEANS (per blank condition) --------------------
  blank_levels <- unique(media_blank_ssf$Conditions)
  
  blank_means <- sapply(blank_levels, function(g) {
    colMeans(media_blank_data[media_blank_ssf$Conditions == g, , drop = FALSE], na.rm = TRUE)
  })
  
  blank_means_df <- as.data.frame(t(blank_means))
  rownames(blank_means_df) <- blank_levels
  
  # IMPORTANT: do NOT convert NA -> 0 here (hides missing blank info)
  
  # -------------------- MATCHING: sample condition -> blank condition --------------------
  pick_blank_group <- function(sample_cond, blank_levels) {
    sample_cond  <- as.character(sample_cond)
    blank_levels <- as.character(blank_levels)
    
    if (is.na(sample_cond) || !nzchar(sample_cond)) return(NA_character_)
    
    s  <- tolower(sample_cond)
    bl <- tolower(blank_levels)
    
    if (s %in% bl) return(blank_levels[match(s, bl)])
    
    hits <- which(vapply(bl, function(b) grepl(b, s, fixed = TRUE) || grepl(s, b, fixed = TRUE), logical(1)))
    
    if (length(hits) == 1) return(blank_levels[hits])
    
    if (length(hits) > 1) {
      lens <- nchar(bl[hits])
      return(blank_levels[hits[which.max(lens)]])
    }
    
    NA_character_
  }
  
  # -------------------- SUBTRACT BLANK MEANS --------------------
  sample_data_corrected <- as.data.frame(
    matrix(NA_real_, nrow = nrow(sample_data), ncol = ncol(sample_data),
           dimnames = list(rownames(sample_data), colnames(sample_data)))
  )
  
  for (i in seq_len(nrow(sample_data))) {
    
    cond <- sample_ssf$Conditions[i]
    
    # If pool: keep as NA (or keep original), but your later code removes pools anyway
    if (!is.na(cond) && tolower(cond) == "pool") {
      sample_data_corrected[i, ] <- NA_real_
      next
    }
    
    bg <- pick_blank_group(cond, rownames(blank_means_df))
    
    if (is.na(bg)) {
      warning(sprintf("No blank group match for sample condition: %s (row %d). Setting row to NA.", cond, i))
      sample_data_corrected[i, ] <- NA_real_
      next
    }
    
    # subtract; keeps NA where sample has NA
    sample_data_corrected[i, ] <- sample_data[i, ] - blank_means_df[bg, ]
  }
  
  # -------------------- AMOUNT NORMALISATION (multiply by 1/amount) --------------------
  SSF <- sample_ssf
  
  # SSF$Amount_numeric <- suppressWarnings(as.numeric(SSF$`Amount of Material`))
  # amounts <- SSF$Amount_numeric
  # names(amounts) <- rownames(SSF)
  # amounts[is.na(amounts) | amounts == 0] <- 1
  # amounts <- 1 / amounts
  
  # align amounts to corrected matrix rows
  # amounts <- amounts[rownames(sample_data_corrected)]
  # amounts[is.na(amounts)] <- 1
  # 
  # # multiply each row by amounts (your intended behaviour)
  # sample_data_corrected <- as.data.frame(sweep(sample_data_corrected, 1, amounts, FUN = "*"))
  
    # align growth table to data by rownames
  gf <- ssf_for_growth_factor[rownames(sample_data_corrected), "Cell Count at Experimental End Point"]

  # n_cells_at_start <- 500000
  # 
  # gf <- gf/n_cells_at_start 
  
  gf[is.na(gf)] <- 1

  
  # divide each sample (row) by its growth factor
  sample_data_corrected <- sweep(sample_data_corrected, 1, gf, FUN = "/")


  
  # -------------------- QC PLOTS (RAW vs BLANK MEAN BARS) --------------------
  pdf(file = file.path(norm_dir, "Media_data_with_CtrlMediaBar2.pdf"), width = 12, height = 5)
  
  for (metab in colnames(sample_data_corrected)) {
    
    plot_df <- tibble(
      Sample = rownames(sample_data_corrected),
      Value  = sample_data[[metab]],          # keeping your original choice (raw shown as points)
      Group  = SSF$Conditions
    )
    
    blank_df <- tibble(
      Group_media = rownames(blank_means_df),
      MeanValue   = blank_means_df[[metab]]
    ) %>%
      mutate(Group = gsub("_control$", "_sample", Group_media))
    
    p1 <- ggplot() +
      geom_col(
        data = blank_df,
        aes(x = Group, y = MeanValue),
        fill = "grey", alpha = 0.6
      ) +
      geom_jitter(
        data = plot_df,
        aes(x = Group, y = Value),
        width = 0.15, size = 2, colour = "blue", alpha = 0.7
      ) +
      theme_bw() +
      labs(title = metab, x = "Condition", y = "Intensity") +
      theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1))
    
    print(p1)
  }
  
  dev.off()
  
  # -------------------- QC PLOTS (RAW vs BLANK MEAN BARS) - NO POOLS --------------------
pdf(file = file.path(norm_dir, "Media_data_with_CtrlMediaBar2_noPools.pdf"),
    width = 12, height = 5)

for (metab in colnames(sample_data_corrected)) {

  plot_df <- tibble::tibble(
    Sample = rownames(sample_data_corrected),
    Value  = sample_data[[metab]],          # raw points (as in your original)
    Group  = SSF$Conditions
  ) %>%
    dplyr::filter(!is.na(Group)) %>%
    dplyr::filter(!grepl("pool", Group, ignore.case = TRUE))  # <-- remove pools

  blank_df <- tibble::tibble(
    Group_media = rownames(blank_means_df),
    MeanValue   = blank_means_df[[metab]]
  ) %>%
    dplyr::mutate(Group = gsub("_control$", "_sample", Group_media))

  # keep only x categories that actually appear (either as sample points or blank bars)
  x_levels <- union(unique(plot_df$Group), unique(blank_df$Group))
  plot_df$Group  <- factor(plot_df$Group, levels = x_levels)
  blank_df$Group <- factor(blank_df$Group, levels = x_levels)

  p1 <- ggplot2::ggplot() +
    ggplot2::geom_col(
      data = blank_df,
      ggplot2::aes(x = Group, y = MeanValue),
      fill = "grey", alpha = 0.6
    ) +
    ggplot2::geom_jitter(
      data = plot_df,
      ggplot2::aes(x = Group, y = Value),
      width = 0.15, size = 2, colour = "blue", alpha = 0.7
    ) +
    ggplot2::theme_bw() +
    ggplot2::labs(title = metab, x = "Condition", y = "Intensity") +
    ggplot2::theme(axis.text.x = ggplot2::element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1))

  print(p1)
}

dev.off()
  
  # ---------- CoRe plotting: corrected data only (no grey bars), zero dotted line ----------
  pdf(file = file.path(norm_dir, "CoRe_CorrectedData_only.pdf"), width = 12, height = 5)
  
  # map sample -> condition (safe even if orders differ)
  cond_map <- SSF$Conditions
  names(cond_map) <- rownames(SSF)
  
  for (metab in colnames(sample_data_corrected)) {
    
    plot_df <- tibble::tibble(
      Sample = rownames(sample_data_corrected),
      Value  = as.numeric(sample_data_corrected[[metab]])
    ) %>%
      dplyr::mutate(Group = unname(cond_map[Sample])) %>%   # <- key fix
      dplyr::filter(!is.na(Group)) %>%
      dplyr::filter(!grepl("pool", Group, ignore.case = TRUE))
    
    p1 <- ggplot2::ggplot(plot_df, ggplot2::aes(x = Group, y = Value)) +
      ggplot2::geom_jitter(width = 0.15, size = 2, colour = "blue", alpha = 0.7) +
      ggplot2::geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dotted", colour = "black") +
      ggplot2::theme_bw() +
      ggplot2::labs(title = metab, x = "Condition", y = "Corrected intensity") +
      ggplot2::theme(axis.text.x = ggplot2::element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1))
    
    print(p1)
  }
  
  dev.off()
  
  # -------------------- OUTPUT OBJECTS --------------------
  
  # remove pools (as in your code)
  SSF <- SSF[-grep("Pool", rownames(SSF), ignore.case = TRUE), , drop = FALSE]
  sample_data_corrected <- sample_data_corrected[-grep("Pool", rownames(sample_data_corrected), ignore.case = TRUE), , drop = FALSE]
  
  make_pca_grid(sample_data_corrected, SSF, add_to_name = "7.5 CoRe_BackgroundSubtracted")
  make_signal_drift_reports(sample_data_corrected, sample_ssf, add_to_name = "7.5 CoRe_BackgroundSubtracted")
}

###  Make postCoRe norm boxplots -------------------
Metab_AllValuePLots <- list()
for (metab in colnames(sample_data_corrected)) {   # metab = colnames(data)[1]
  # Merge metabolite values with metadata
  plotdata <- merge(sample_data_corrected %>% select(all_of(metab)),  SSF, by = 0)
  
  # Ensure RunOrder is numeric and sort
 # plotdata <- plotdata[order(plotdata$RunOrder), ]
  
  # Make Row.names a factor ordered by RunOrder
  plotdata$Row.names <- factor(plotdata$Row.names, levels = plotdata$Row.names)
  
  # Plot with Colors column used for custom colors but Conditions for legend
  p <- ggplot(plotdata, aes(x = Row.names, y = .data[[metab]], fill = Conditions)) +
    geom_col(position = "dodge") +
    scale_fill_manual(values = setNames(plotdata$Color, plotdata$Conditions)) + # map colors
    labs(title = paste(metab),
         x = "Samples (Run Order)", y = "Intensity") +
    theme_minimal() +
    theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1, size = 6),  # rotate x labels
          plot.title = element_text(size = 14))
  
  # Store plot
  Metab_AllValuePLots[[metab]] <- p
}

# Save blank Bar-plots
AllValueBoxplotPreMVIloc <- file.path(output.dir,paste0("Metabolite_AllValues_postCoRenorm_boxplots.pdf"))
pdf(AllValueBoxplotPreMVIloc, width = 14, height = 8) # Adjust width and height as needed

# Iterate over the list of plots and print each one
for (plot in Metab_AllValuePLots) {
  print(plot)
}

# Close the PDF device
dev.off()

MVI_data <- sample_data_corrected 
paste("# CoRe normalisation using media blanks Finished!")
```


### --- 3. Data preparation/finalisation
#3.0 Preparation
```{r}
# Set sub folders
Data_finalisation_dir <- file.path(output.dir, "3. Data Finalisation")
if (!dir.exists(Data_finalisation_dir)) dir.create(Data_finalisation_dir, recursive = TRUE)

# Metabolite_Metadata$`SNratio<3` <- rownames(Metabolite_Metadata) %in% gsub("[^A-Za-z0-9 ]", "", names(blank_dominated_features[blank_dominated_features]))
# Data_cleaning_dir
# , ProjectCode,"_",Sys.Date(), 
paste("#3.0 Preparation Finished!")
```

#3.1 Data transformation
```{r}
if(CoRe==F){
  data <- log2(MVI_data) 
}else{
  data <-MVI_data
}
# PCA
PCA_data <- data
PCA_SSF <- SSF
make_pca_grid(PCA_data, PCA_SSF, add_to_name = "8. transformed")
make_signal_drift_reports(PCA_data, PCA_SSF, add_to_name = "8. transformed")
  
paste("#3.1 Data transformation Finished!")
```

#3.2. Outlier samples
```{r}
SSFsave <- SSF
# SSF <- SSFsave
initial_data <- data
data_norm <- data
Outlier_filtering_loop <- 2

sample_outliers <- list()
outlier_plot_list <- list()

for (loop in 1:Outlier_filtering_loop) {
  PCA.res <- prcomp(data_norm, center = TRUE, scale. = TRUE)
  
  # Scree plot data
  inflect_df <- data.frame(x = 1:length(PCA.res$sdev))
  inflect_df$y <- summary(PCA.res)$importance[2, ]
  inflect_df$Cumulative <- summary(PCA.res)$importance[3, ]
  screeplot_cumul <- format(round(inflect_df$Cumulative[1:20]*100, 1), nsmall = 1)
  knee <- inflection::uik(inflect_df$x, inflect_df$y)
  npcs <- knee - 1
  if(npcs <= 1) { npcs <- 2 }
  
  # Scree plot
  screeplot <- factoextra::fviz_screeplot(PCA.res, main = paste("PCA Explained variance plot filtering round ", loop),
                                          addlabels = TRUE, ncp = 20, geom = c("bar", "line"),
                                          barfill = "grey", barcolor = "grey", linecolor = "black") +
    theme_classic() +
    geom_vline(xintercept = npcs + 0.5, linetype = 2, color = "red") +
    annotate("text", x = 1:20, y = -0.8, label = screeplot_cumul, col = "black", size = 3)
  
  
  outlier_plot_list[[paste0("Scree_", loop)]] <-screeplot
  
  
  # PCA plot PC1 vs PC2
  pca_df <- data.frame(Sample = rownames(PCA.res$x), PCA.res$x[, 1:2], Group = SSF$Conditions)
  colnames(pca_df)[2:3] <- c("PC1", "PC2")
  
  pc1_var <- round(100 * summary(PCA.res)$importance[2, 1], 1)
  pc2_var <- round(100 * summary(PCA.res)$importance[2, 2], 1)
  
  pca_plot <- ggplot(pca_df, aes(x = PC1, y = PC2, color = Group)) +
    geom_point(size = 3) +
    geom_text_repel(aes(label = Sample), size = 3, max.overlaps = 100) +
    scale_color_manual(values = setNames(SSF$Color, SSF$Conditions)) +
    labs(title = paste("PCA Score Plot Round", loop),
         x = paste0("PC1 (", pc1_var, "%)"),
         y = paste0("PC2 (", pc2_var, "%)")) +
    theme_classic()
  
  outlier_plot_list[[paste0("PCA_", loop)]] <-pca_plot
  
  # Hotelling's T2 test
  data_hot <- PCA.res$x[, 1:npcs]
  qcc_res <- qcc::mqcc(data_hot, type = "T2.single", labels = rownames(data_hot), confidence.level = 0.99, plot = FALSE)
  limits <- as.data.frame(qcc_res$limits)
  t2_data <- data.frame(Samples = names(qcc_res$statistics), Statistic = qcc_res$statistics)
  outliers <- t2_data[t2_data$Statistic > limits$UCL, , drop = FALSE]
  
  # Hotelling plot
  hotelling_plot <- ggplot(t2_data, aes(x = Samples, y = Statistic)) +
    geom_point(color = "blue", size = 2) +
    geom_point(data = outliers, aes(x = Samples, y = Statistic), color = "red", size = 3) +
    geom_line(group = 1, linetype = 2) +
    geom_hline(yintercept =limits[1,2], color = "red", linetype = "dashed") +
    labs(title = paste("Hotelling T2 Test Round", loop), y = "Group Summary Statistic") +
    theme_classic() +
    theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1))
  
  outlier_plot_list[[paste0("Hotelling_", loop)]] <- hotelling_plot
  
  # Remove outliers
  if (nrow(outliers) == 0) break
  data_norm <- data_norm[!rownames(data_norm) %in% outliers$Samples, , drop = FALSE]
  SSF <- SSF[!rownames(SSF) %in% outliers$Samples,]
  sample_outliers[[paste0("Round_", loop)]] <- outliers$Samples
}


Outlierloc <- file.path(Data_finalisation_dir,paste0("Outlier_testing" ,".pdf"))
pdf(Outlierloc, width = 8, height = 8) # Adjust width and height as needed

# Iterate over the list of plots and print each one
for (plot in outlier_plot_list) {
  print(plot)
}
# Close the PDF device
dev.off()

# Export outliers
if (length(sample_outliers) > 0) {
  sink(file.path(Data_finalisation_dir, "Outliers.txt"))
  print(sample_outliers)
  sink()
}

# data_norm <- sample_data_corrected

# # remove manually
# remove <- "ACNe01_Cells_017"
# data_norm <- data_norm[!rownames(data_norm) %in% remove, , drop = FALSE]
# SSF <- SSF[!rownames(SSF) %in% remove,]

# PCA
PCA_data <- data_norm
PCA_SSF <- SSF
res <- make_pca_grid(PCA_data, PCA_SSF, add_to_name = "9. Outlier_removed")
make_signal_drift_reports(PCA_data, PCA_SSF, add_to_name = "9. Outlier_removed")

paste("#3.2. Outlier samples Finished!")
```

# Merge technical replicates
```{r eval=FALSE}

data <- data_norm   # or MVI_data
SSF2 <- SSF

# align
common <- intersect(rownames(data), rownames(SSF2))
data <- data[common, , drop = FALSE]
SSF2 <- SSF2[common, , drop = FALSE]

# keep factor order
SSF2$Conditions <- factor(SSF2$Conditions, levels = levels(SSF$Conditions))

# -------------------- MERGE TECH REPS (median) --------------------
SSF2_tbl <- SSF2 %>% rownames_to_column("SampleID")

merged_tbl <- SSF2_tbl %>%
  group_by(Conditions, `Biological Replicate`) %>%
  summarise(
    merged = list(apply(data[SampleID, , drop = FALSE], 2, median, na.rm = TRUE)),
    Color  = dplyr::first(Color),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  mutate(NewSampleID = paste0(Conditions, "_BioRep", `Biological Replicate`))

merged_data <- do.call(rbind, merged_tbl$merged)
rownames(merged_data) <- merged_tbl$NewSampleID
merged_data <- as.data.frame(merged_data)

merged_SSF <- merged_tbl %>%
  select(NewSampleID, Conditions, `Biological Replicate`, Color) %>%
  column_to_rownames("NewSampleID")

merged_SSF$Conditions

# -------------------- QC PLOTS (before vs merged) --------------------
pdf(file.path(output.dir, "Merge_TechReps_QCplots.pdf"), width = 10, height = 6)

for (metab in colnames(data)) {
  
  df_raw <- SSF2_tbl %>%
    mutate(
      Value = data[SampleID, metab],
      Type = "Raw"
    ) %>%
    select(SampleID, Conditions, `Biological Replicate`, Value, Type)
  
  df_merged <- merged_SSF %>%
    rownames_to_column("SampleID") %>%
    mutate(
      Value = merged_data[SampleID, metab],
      Type = "Merged"
    ) %>%
    select(SampleID, Conditions, `Biological Replicate`, Value, Type)
  
  df_plot <- bind_rows(df_raw, df_merged) %>%
    mutate(Type = factor(Type, levels = c("Raw", "Merged")))
  
  p <- ggplot(df_plot, aes(x = Type, y = Value, fill = Type)) +
    geom_boxplot(outlier.shape = NA, alpha = 0.6) +
    geom_jitter(width = 0.15, size = 1.8, alpha = 0.7) +
    facet_grid(`Biological Replicate` ~ Conditions, scales = "free_y") +
    theme_bw() +
    labs(title = metab, x = "", y = "Intensity") +
    theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1))
  
  print(p)
}

dev.off()

paste("#3.3 Technical Replicate Merging Finished!")
```


#3.3 Finalise data processing
```{r}
suppressMessages(in_data <- readxl::read_excel(file_listTF[grep("ssf", tolower(file_listTF), invert = T)], sheet = 1, col_names = T) |> as.data.frame()) # load data file
rownames(in_data) <- in_data[[1]]   # set first column as row names
in_data <- in_data[, -1]     
write.csv(in_data, paste0(Data_finalisation_dir, "/Raw_Data.csv"))


processed_data <- data_norm
#processed_data$`valine-d8` <- NULL
processed_data <- processed_data[,-grep(valine_pattern_id, tolower(colnames(processed_data)))]

processed_data <- processed_data[!grepl("pool", tolower(rownames(processed_data))),]
processed_data[sapply(processed_data, is.numeric)] <- round(processed_data[sapply(processed_data, is.numeric)], 3)

out_data <- processed_data
SSF <- SSF[rownames(out_data),]

write.csv(out_data, paste0(Data_finalisation_dir, "/Processed_Data.csv"))


if (exists("merge_tectno_Reps") && isTRUE(merge_tectno_Reps)) {
  SSF <- merged_SSF
  
  processed_data <- data_norm
#processed_data$`valine-d8` <- NULL
processed_data <- processed_data[,-grep(valine_pattern_id, tolower(colnames(processed_data)))]

processed_data <- processed_data[!grepl("pool", tolower(rownames(processed_data))),]
processed_data[sapply(processed_data, is.numeric)] <- round(processed_data[sapply(processed_data, is.numeric)], 3)

out_data <- processed_data
SSF <- SSF[rownames(out_data),]

write.csv(out_data, paste0(Data_finalisation_dir, "/Processed_Data_unmerged.csv"))

processed_data <- merged_data
#processed_data$`valine-d8` <- NULL
processed_data <- processed_data[,-grep(valine_pattern_id, tolower(colnames(processed_data)))]

processed_data <- processed_data[!grepl("pool", tolower(rownames(processed_data))),]
processed_data[sapply(processed_data, is.numeric)] <- round(processed_data[sapply(processed_data, is.numeric)], 3)

out_data <- processed_data
merged_SSF <- merged_SSF[rownames(out_data),]

write.csv(out_data, paste0(Data_finalisation_dir, "/Processed_Data.csv"))
}

# PCA
PCA_data <- processed_data
PCA_SSF <- SSF
res <- make_pca_grid(PCA_data, PCA_SSF, add_to_name = "10. Processed_data")

#make_signal_drift_reports(PCA_data, PCA_SSF, add_to_name = "10. Processed_data")

paste("#3.3 Finalise data processing Finished!")
```


# CHeck if BIOlogical vairation is in first 2 compoents
```{r eval = FALSE}

# -------------------- INPUT --------------------
X <- as.matrix(processed_data)          # samples in rows, features in columns
meta <- SSF                     # sample metadata; rownames must match MVI_data

# keep only common samples + align
common <- intersect(rownames(X), rownames(meta))
X <- X[common, , drop = FALSE]
meta <- meta[common, , drop = FALSE]

# optional: log transform (skip if already log)
# X <- log10(X + 1)

# remove rows with any NA / Inf
keep <- is.finite(rowSums(X)) & !apply(X, 1, function(r) any(!is.finite(r)) || any(is.na(r)))
X <- X[keep, , drop = FALSE]
meta <- meta[keep, , drop = FALSE]

# -------------------- PCA --------------------
pca <- prcomp(X, center = TRUE, scale. = TRUE)

scores <- as.data.frame(pca$x[, 1:3])
scores$Sample <- rownames(scores)

df <- scores %>%
  left_join(meta %>% rownames_to_column("Sample"), by = "Sample")

# % variance for axis labels
pc_var <- round(100 * (pca$sdev^2 / sum(pca$sdev^2)), 2)

# -------------------- PLOT --------------------
p <- ggplot(df, aes(PC1, PC2,
                    fill  = factor(`Biological Replicate`),
                    shape = Conditions)) +
  geom_point(size = 5, stroke = 1, color = "black") +
  scale_shape_manual(values = rep(c(21,22,23,24,25),
                                  length.out = length(unique(df$Conditions)))) +
  guides(
    fill = guide_legend(override.aes = list(shape = 21, color = "black", size = 5))
  ) +
  theme_classic() +
  labs(
    fill  = "Biological Replicate",
    shape = "Condition",
    x = paste0("PC1 (", pc_var[1], "%)"),
    y = paste0("PC2 (", pc_var[2], "%)")
  )

ggsave(file.path(output.dir, "PCA_BiologicalReplicate.svg"),
       plot = p, width = 12, height = 10)
```

### ----- 4. Data Visualisations ----- 
#4.1. Final PCA
```{r}
#update frpm original  colors
customColors <- customColorsBox

data <-processed_data

# --- 1. Define color column and ensure output folder exists ---
cols_to_color <- colnames(SSF)[1]

# (Optional) skip columns with too many unique values (e.g., IDs)
# cols_to_color <- cols_to_color[sapply(cols_to_color, function(c)
#   length(unique(SSF[[c]])) <= 30)]

# Run your function for each SSF column
for (cc in cols_to_color) {
  make_pca_grid(
    data        = data,
    SSF         = SSF,
    add_to_name = paste0("Final PCA_by_", cc),
    project_code= ProjectCode,
    color_col   = cc,
    fig_size_cm = c(60, 40)
  )
}


# --- 3. Perform PCA once on all samples (Pools included) ---
pca_res <- prcomp(data, scale. = TRUE)
pca_var_explained <- summary(pca_res)$importance[2, 1:5] * 100  # % for PC1–PC5
SSF$Label <- gsub(paste0(ProjectCode, "_"), "", rownames(SSF))  # Sample names for plotting

# --- 6. Final PCA plot with PC1 vs PC2 excluding Pools ---
non_pool <- !grepl("pool", tolower(SSF$Conditions))
pca_df <- data.frame(pca_res$x[non_pool, 1:2],
                     Conditions = SSF$Conditions[non_pool],
                     Label = SSF$Label[non_pool])
colnames(pca_df)[1:2] <- c("PC1", "PC2")

pc1_var <- round(100 * summary(pca_res)$importance[2, 1], 1)
pc2_var <- round(100 * summary(pca_res)$importance[2, 2], 1)

# make sure factor levels match palette order (optional but tidy)
cond_levels <- intersect(unique(pca_df$Conditions), customColors$Conditions)
pca_df$Conditions <- factor(pca_df$Conditions, levels = cond_levels)
pal_use <- customColors$Color[match(cond_levels, customColors$Conditions)]

final_pca_plot <- ggplot(pca_df, aes(x = PC1, y = PC2, color = Conditions)) +
  geom_point(size = 3) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, color = "black", linewidth = 0.2) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, color = "black", linewidth = 0.2) +
  scale_color_manual(values = pal_use) +
  labs(title = "PCA: PC1 vs PC2 by Condition", x = paste0("PC1 (", pc1_var, "%)"),
       y = paste0("PC2 (", pc2_var, "%)")) +
  theme_classic()


ggsave(file.path(file.path(output.dir, "PCA_plots"), "Final_PCA_by_Conditions.svg"),
       plot = final_pca_plot, width = 20, height = 15, units = "cm")


# --- Add names to final PCA ---

final_pca_plot <- ggplot(pca_df, aes(x = PC1, y = PC2, color = Conditions)) +
  geom_point(size = 3) +
  ggrepel::geom_text_repel(aes(label = Label), size = 3, show.legend = FALSE) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, color = "black", linewidth = 0.2) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, color = "black", linewidth = 0.2) +
  scale_color_manual(values = pal_use) +
  labs(
    title = "PCA: PC1 vs PC2 by Condition",
    x = paste0("PC1 (", pc1_var, "%)"),
    y = paste0("PC2 (", pc2_var, "%)")
  ) +
  theme_classic()

ggsave(file.path(file.path(output.dir, "PCA_plots"), "Final_PCA_by_Conditions_Labeled.svg"),
       plot = final_pca_plot, width = 20, height = 15, units = "cm")
paste("#4.1. Final PCA Finished!")
```

#4.2. Heatmap
```{r} 
# Custom scaling function: scale values to [-1, 1]
scale_to_minus_one_to_one <- function(vec) {
  if (max(vec, na.rm = TRUE) == min(vec, na.rm = TRUE)) {
    return(rep(0, length(vec)))  # If constant, return 0s
  }
  2 * (vec - min(vec, na.rm = TRUE)) / (max(vec, na.rm = TRUE) - min(vec, na.rm = TRUE)) - 1
}

# Scale data
scaled_data <- as.data.frame(lapply(processed_data, scale_to_minus_one_to_one))   # or out_data
colnames(scaled_data) <- colnames(processed_data)
rownames(scaled_data) <- rownames(processed_data)

# Remove pools
# scaled_data <- scaled_data[-grep("pool", tolower(rownames(scaled_data))),]

# --------------------- CHANGED ANNOTATION ONLY ---------------------

# Sample annotation (align to heatmap columns = rownames(scaled_data))
annotation_col <- data.frame(Condition = SSF$Conditions, row.names = rownames(SSF))
annotation_col <- annotation_col[rownames(scaled_data), , drop = FALSE]
annotation_col$Condition <- as.factor(annotation_col$Condition)

# Build color mapping from SSF$Color
pal_df   <- unique(SSF[, c("Conditions", "Color")])
cond_pal <- setNames(pal_df$Color, pal_df$Conditions)   # named vector: names = condition, values = color

annotation_colors <- list(Condition = cond_pal)

# ------------------------------------------------------------------

# Feature annotation with safe rownames
clean_for_match <- function(x) gsub("[*†]", "", x)

orig_names  <- colnames(scaled_data)           # keep symbols for display
clean_names <- clean_for_match(orig_names)     # remove symbols for matching

# Match to universe
annotation_row <- data.frame(MainClass = universe$`Main.class`)
rownames(annotation_row) <- universe$`Input.name`
annotation_row <- annotation_row[clean_names, , drop = FALSE]

# Assign original names with symbols as rownames (display)
rownames(annotation_row) <- orig_names

# Ensure annotation_row only contains rows matching the final matrix
annotation_row <- annotation_row[colnames(scaled_data), , drop = FALSE]

# Plot
svg(paste0(output.dir, "/Heatmap_scaled.svg"), width = 10, height = 22)
set.seed(1234)
pheatmap(
  mat = t(as.matrix(scaled_data)),
  clustering_method = "ward.D2",
  clustering_distance_rows = "euclidean",
  clustering_distance_cols = "euclidean",
  annotation_col = annotation_col,
  annotation_row = annotation_row,
  annotation_colors = annotation_colors,
  main = "Metabolite heatmap",
  angle_col = 45,
  fontsize_col = 8,
  fontsize_row = 6
)
dev.off()

# ---------------- HEATMAP 2 (samples grouped by SSF$Conditions levels) ----------------

# enforce group order = factor levels
annotation_col$Condition <- factor(annotation_col$Condition, levels = levels(SSF$Conditions))

# order samples by Condition, keeping within-group order as-is
ord <- order(annotation_col$Condition)
scaled_data2 <- scaled_data[ord, , drop = FALSE]
annotation_col2 <- annotation_col[ord, , drop = FALSE]

svg(paste0(output.dir, "/Heatmap_Unclustered_Condition.svg"), width = 10, height = 22)
set.seed(1234)
pheatmap(
  mat = t(as.matrix(scaled_data2)),
  cluster_rows = TRUE,     # <-- only change: remove row clustering too
  cluster_cols = FALSE,
  clustering_method = "ward.D2",
  clustering_distance_rows = "euclidean",
  # clustering_distance_cols = "euclidean",
  annotation_col = annotation_col2,
  annotation_row = annotation_row,
  annotation_colors = annotation_colors,
  main = "Metabolite heatmap Unclustered Conditions",
  angle_col = 45,
  fontsize_col = 8,
  fontsize_row = 6
)
dev.off()

paste("#4.2. Heatmap Finished!")

```

#4.3. Boxplots
```{r} 
Box_data <- processed_data #processed_data # the log2 data --> out_data

Box_data <- merge( SSF %>%select(Conditions),Box_data, by=0)
Box_data <- column_to_rownames(Box_data, "Row.names")
Box_data <- Box_data[!grepl("Pool", Box_data$Conditions),]
Box_data$Conditions <- factor(Box_data$Conditions,levels =   unique(unique(SSF$Conditions)  )) # Merge for boxplots we use the SSSF check it out its potential reason for error!!
boxplot_colors <- customColorsBox[match(levels(Box_data$Conditions), customColorsBox$Conditions), ]

plot_list <- list()
for (i in 2:ncol(Box_data)){ # i=4
  message( colnames(Box_data[i]))
  
  boxplotdata <- Box_data %>%  select(i,1) %>%                       
    group_by(Conditions)
  NAMES <- names(boxplotdata)[1]
  message("working metabolite: ",NAMES)
  names(boxplotdata)[1] <- c("Intensity")
  
  
  extract <- boxplotdata %>% as.data.frame()
  extract$Intensity <- as.numeric(extract$Intensity)
  
  #extract$Conditions <- factor(extract$Conditions, levels = names(boxplot_colors))
  box_base <- ggbetweenstats(data = extract,
                             x = Conditions,
                             y = Intensity,
                             bf.message = FALSE,
                             title = paste(NAMES),
                             results.subtitle = FALSE,
                             pairwise.comparisons = FALSE,
                             pairwise.display = "none",
                             ylab = "normalised intensity"
  )
  
  # Force y-axis to start at 0
  boxplot <- box_base +
    geom_hline(yintercept = 0, colour = "black", linetype = "dotted") +
    ggplot2::scale_color_manual(values = c(boxplot_colors$Color))+
    # Unsilence the line below to stop forcing zero on y
    #scale_y_continuous(limits = range(extract$Intensity, na.rm = TRUE))+
    theme(
      plot.title = element_text(size = 22),
      plot.subtitle = element_text(size = 10),
      legend.title = element_text(size = 20),
      legend.text = element_text(size = 20),
      axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1, vjust = 1, size = 10)
    )

  

  
  plot_list[NAMES][[1]] <- boxplot #final_plot 
}


Boxplotloc <- file.path(output.dir,paste0("Boxplots_Y=0" ,".pdf"))
pdf(Boxplotloc, width = 8, height = 8) # Adjust width and height as needed

# Iterate over the list of plots and print each one
for (plot in plot_list) {
  print(plot)
}

# Close the PDF device
dev.off()



plot_list <- list()
for (i in 2:ncol(Box_data)){ # i=4
  message( colnames(Box_data[i]))
  
  boxplotdata <- Box_data %>%  select(i,1) %>%                       
    group_by(Conditions)
  NAMES <- names(boxplotdata)[1]
  message("working metabolite: ",NAMES)
  names(boxplotdata)[1] <- c("Intensity")
  
  
  extract <- boxplotdata %>% as.data.frame()
  extract$Intensity <- as.numeric(extract$Intensity)
  
  #extract$Conditions <- factor(extract$Conditions, levels = names(boxplot_colors))
  box_base <- ggbetweenstats(data = extract,
                             x = Conditions,
                             y = Intensity,
                             bf.message = FALSE,
                             title = paste(NAMES),
                             results.subtitle = FALSE,
                             pairwise.comparisons = FALSE,
                             pairwise.display = "none",
                             ylab = "normalised intensity"
  )
  
  # Force y-axis to start at 0
  boxplot <- box_base +
    geom_hline(yintercept = 0, colour = "black", linetype = "dotted") +
    ggplot2::scale_color_manual(values = c(boxplot_colors$Color))+
    # Unsilence the line below to stop forcing zero on y
    scale_y_continuous(limits = range(extract$Intensity, na.rm = TRUE))+
    theme(
      plot.title = element_text(size = 22),
      plot.subtitle = element_text(size = 10),
      legend.title = element_text(size = 20),
      legend.text = element_text(size = 20),
      axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1, vjust = 1, size = 10)
    )

  

  
  plot_list[NAMES][[1]] <- boxplot #final_plot 
}


Boxplotloc <- file.path(output.dir,paste0("Boxplots" ,".pdf"))
pdf(Boxplotloc, width = 8, height = 8) # Adjust width and height as needed

# Iterate over the list of plots and print each one
for (plot in plot_list) {
  print(plot)
}

# Close the PDF device
dev.off()
paste("#4.3. Boxplots Finished!")
```



### ------ 5. Data Analysis ------
#5.1.DMA and Ora parameter setting
```{r}
DMA_dir <- file.path(output.dir, "4. DMA")
if (!dir.exists(DMA_dir)) dir.create(DMA_dir, recursive = TRUE)

unique(SSF$Conditions[match(rownames(processed_data), rownames(SSF))])
# 3. Get two groups
group1 <-"Type_2"   # Change as needed
group2 <- "Vehicle"   # Change as needed

comp_name <- paste0(group1, "_Vs_", group2)

subdir_path <- file.path(DMA_dir, comp_name)
# 2. Create the subdirectory if it doesn't exist
if (!dir.exists(subdir_path)) {
  dir.create(subdir_path, recursive = TRUE)
}
# 3. Set as working directory
setwd(subdir_path)
```

# t-test
```{r}
# 1. Log2 transform the data
#dma_data <- log2(processed_data)
dma_data <- processed_data

# 2. Match sample conditions
Conditions <- SSF$Conditions[match(rownames(dma_data), rownames(SSF))]

# 4. Filter sample indices
idx1 <- which(Conditions == group1)
idx2 <- which(Conditions == group2)

# 5. Perform t-test for each metabolite
results <- apply(dma_data, 2, function(x) {
  x1 <- x[idx1]
  x2 <- x[idx2]
  
  if(CoRe==T){
    mean1 <- mean(x1, na.rm = TRUE)
    mean2 <- mean(x2, na.rm = TRUE)
    
    NA0 <- any(x1 == 0, na.rm = TRUE) | any(x2 == 0, na.rm = TRUE)
    
    if(abs(mean1 - mean2)<=1){
      log2dist <- 0.1
    }else{
      log2dist <- log2(abs(mean1 - mean2))
    }

    if (abs(mean1) > abs(mean2)) log2dist <- log2dist * 1
    if (abs(mean1) < abs(mean2)) log2dist <- log2dist * -1
    
    if (mean1 > 0 & mean2 > 0) core_spec <- "Released"
    else if (mean1 < 0 & mean2 < 0) core_spec <- "Consumed"
    else if (mean1 > 0 & mean2 < 0) core_spec <- paste("Released in", group1, "and Consumed", group2)
    else if (mean1 < 0 & mean2 > 0) core_spec <- paste("Consumed in", group1, "and Released", group2)
    else core_spec <- "No Change"
    
    core_class <- ifelse(core_spec == "Released", "Released",
                         ifelse(core_spec == "Consumed", "Consumed", "Released/Consumed"))
    
    pval <- tryCatch(t.test(x1, x2)$p.value, error = function(e) NA)
    
    return(c(Log2_Distance = log2dist,
             p.val = pval,
             CoRe_specific = core_spec,
             CoRe = core_class,
             Mean_group1 = mean1,
             Mean_group2 = mean2))
  }else{
    pval <- tryCatch(t.test(x1, x2)$p.value, error = function(e) NA)
    log2FC <- mean(x1, na.rm = TRUE) - mean(x2, na.rm = TRUE)
    c(log2FC = log2FC, p.val = pval)
  }
  
})

# 6. Build final dataframe
DMA_results_df <- as.data.frame(t(results))
DMA_results_df$feature <- rownames(DMA_results_df)
DMA_results_df$p.adjusted <- p.adjust(DMA_results_df$p.val, method = "fdr")

# 7. Prepare output and save
if(CoRe==T){
  DMA_results_df <- DMA_results_df[, c("feature", "Log2_Distance", "p.val", "p.adjusted", "CoRe_specific", "CoRe",          "Mean_group1" ,  "Mean_group2" )]
  colnames(DMA_results_df)[1] <- "Metabolite"
  DMA_results_df$Log2_Distance <- as.numeric(DMA_results_df$Log2_Distance)
  #DMA_results_df[] <- lapply(DMA_results_df, function(x) if(is.numeric(x)) round(x, 4) else x)
  #
 # DMA_results_df[sapply(DMA_results_df, is.numeric)] <- round(DMA_results_df[sapply(DMA_results_df, is.numeric)], 8)
  DMA_results_df$Log2_Distance <- round(DMA_results_df$Log2_Distance, 3)
  write.csv(DMA_results_df, file = file.path(subdir_path, paste0("CoRe_t.test_", comp_name, ".csv")), row.names = FALSE)
  
}else{
  DMA_results_df <- DMA_results_df[, c("feature", "log2FC", "p.val", "p.adjusted")]
  colnames(DMA_results_df)[1] <- "Metabolite"
  #DMA_results_df[] <- lapply(DMA_results_df, function(x) if(is.numeric(x)) round(x, 4) else x)
  #DMA_results_df[sapply(DMA_results_df, is.numeric)] <- round(DMA_results_df[sapply(DMA_results_df, is.numeric)], 8)
  
  # num_mat <- as.matrix(DMA_results_df[sapply(DMA_results_df, is.numeric)])
  # min_val <- min(num_mat[num_mat > 0], na.rm = TRUE)
  # dec <- ceiling(-log10(min_val))
  # DMA_results_df[sapply(DMA_results_df, is.numeric)] <- round(DMA_results_df[sapply(DMA_results_df, is.numeric)], dec)


  DMA_results_df$log2FC <- round(DMA_results_df$log2FC, 3)
  write.csv(DMA_results_df, file = file.path(paste0(subdir_path, "/t.test_",comp_name,".csv")), row.names = FALSE)
}


DMA_results_df$feature <- DMA_results_df$Metabolite
DMA_results_df$p.val <- as.numeric(DMA_results_df$p.val)

```

# p Cut off selection
```{r}
DMA_results_df$neglog10_pval <- -log10(DMA_results_df$p.adjusted)

fc_cutoff <- 0.5
pval_cutoff =0.05


p.cutoff_selection <- ggplot(DMA_results_df, aes(x = reorder(feature, neglog10_pval), y = neglog10_pval)) +
  geom_col(fill = "darkred") +
  geom_hline(yintercept = c(-log10(0.05), -log10(0.01), -log10(0.001), -log10(pval_cutoff)), 
             linetype = "dashed", color = "blue") +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", formula = y ~ poly(x, 2), se = FALSE, color = "blue", size = 1) +  # Quadratic fit
  annotate("text", x = Inf, y = -log10(0.05), label = "p = 0.05", hjust = 12, vjust = -0.3, color = "blue") +
  annotate("text", x = Inf, y = -log10(0.01), label = "p = 0.01", hjust = 12, vjust = -0.3, color = "blue") +
  annotate("text", x = Inf, y = -log10(0.001), label = "p = 0.001", hjust = 10.4, vjust = -0.3, color = "blue") +
  annotate("text", x = Inf, y = -log10(pval_cutoff), label = paste0("p = ", pval_cutoff), hjust = 14.1, vjust = -0.3, color = "red") +
  labs(title = "-log10(p-value) by Feature", x = "Metabolite", y = "-log10(p-value)") +
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_blank(),
    axis.ticks.x = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.major.x = element_blank()
  )

p.cutoff_selection

ggsave(paste0(subdir_path, "/p.adj_cutoff_selection.svg"), plot = p.cutoff_selection, width = 10, height = 8)


# 
# # Order data by p-value
# DMA_results_df <- DMA_results_df[order(DMA_results_df$neglog10_pval), ]
# DMA_results_df$feature <- factor(DMA_results_df$feature, levels = DMA_results_df$feature)
# DMA_results_df$feature_index <- seq_len(nrow(DMA_results_df))  # numeric for fitting
# 
# ggplot(DMA_results_df, aes(x = feature, y = neglog10_pval)) +
#   geom_col(fill = "darkred") +
#   geom_line(aes(x = feature, y = predict(lm(neglog10_pval ~ poly(feature_index, 2), data = DMA_results_df))),
#             color = "black", size = 1, group = 1) +
#   geom_hline(yintercept = c(-log10(0.001), -log10(0.01), -log10(0.05), -log10(x)),
#              linetype = "dashed", color = "blue") +
#   annotate("text", x = 1, y = -log10(0.001), label = "p = 0.001", hjust = -0.2, color = "blue") +
#   annotate("text", x = 1, y = -log10(0.01),  label = "p = 0.01",  hjust = -0.2, color = "blue") +
#   annotate("text", x = 1, y = -log10(0.05),  label = "p = 0.05",  hjust = -0.2, color = "blue") +
#   annotate("text", x = 1, y = -log10(x),     label = "p = 0.1",   hjust = -0.2, color = "red") +
#   labs(title = "-log10(p-value) by Feature with Quadratic Fit",
#        x = "Metabolite", y = "-log10(p-value)") +
#   theme(
#     axis.text.x = element_blank(),
#     axis.ticks.x = element_blank(),
#     panel.grid.major.x = element_blank()
#   )



```

# Volcanos 
```{r}
# Assuming your results are in a data.frame like this:
# DMA_results_df: columns = feature, log2FC, p.val, p.adjusted

if(CoRe==T){
  
  DMA_results_df$Significance <- ifelse(DMA_results_df$p.adjusted < pval_cutoff &
                                          abs(DMA_results_df$Log2_Distance) > fc_cutoff,
                                        ifelse(DMA_results_df$Log2_Distance > 0, "Up", "Down"),
                                        "NS")
  
  #DMA CORE PLOT
  p2 <-ggplot(DMA_results_df, aes(x = Log2_Distance, y = neglog10_pval, color = CoRe)) +
    geom_point(size = 2) +
    geom_text_repel(
      data = subset(DMA_results_df, Significance != "NS"),
      aes(label = feature),
      size = 3,
      max.overlaps = 50,
      box.padding = 0.3,
      segment.color = "grey50"
    ) +
    geom_hline(yintercept = -log10(pval_cutoff), linetype = "dashed", color = "black") +
    geom_vline(xintercept = c(-fc_cutoff, fc_cutoff), linetype = "dashed", color = "black") +
    scale_color_manual(values = c(
      "Released" = "blue",
      "Consumed" = "green",
      "Released/Consumed" = "purple",
      "No Change" = "grey"
    )) +
    labs(
      title = paste(group1, "vs", group2),
      x = "Log2 Distance",
      y = "-Log10 Adjusted p-value"
    ) +
    theme_minimal(base_size = 14)
  
  
  ggsave(file.path(subdir_path, paste0("Volcano_CoRe_", comp_name, ".svg")), plot = p2, width = 10, height = 8)
  
  # DMA CORE ZOOMED PLOT
  p2_zoom <- ggplot(DMA_results_df, aes(x = Log2_Distance, y = neglog10_pval, color = CoRe)) +
    geom_point(size = 2) +
    geom_text_repel(
      data = subset(DMA_results_df, Significance != "NS"),
      aes(label = feature),
      size = 3,
      max.overlaps = 50,
      box.padding = 0.3,
      segment.color = "grey50"
    ) +
    geom_hline(yintercept = -log10(pval_cutoff), linetype = "dashed", color = "black") +
    geom_vline(xintercept = c(-fc_cutoff, fc_cutoff), linetype = "dashed", color = "black") +
    scale_color_manual(values = c(
      "Released" = "blue",
      "Consumed" = "green",
      "Released/Consumed" = "purple",
      "No Change" = "grey"
    )) +
    labs(
      title = paste(group1, "vs", group2),
      x = "Log2 Distance",
      y = "-Log10 Adjusted p-value"
    ) +
    theme_minimal(base_size = 14)
  
  ggsave(file.path(subdir_path, paste0("Volcano_CoRe_", comp_name, "_ZOOM.svg")),
         plot = p2_zoom,  width = 28, height = 18, limitsize = FALSE)
  
  
  
  
  #DMA CORE PLOT
  p1 <-ggplot(DMA_results_df, aes(x = Log2_Distance, y = neglog10_pval)) +
    geom_point(aes(color = Significance), size = 2) +
    geom_text_repel(
      data = subset(DMA_results_df, Significance != "NS"),
      aes(label = feature),
      size = 3,
      max.overlaps = 50,
      box.padding = 0.3,
      segment.color = "grey50"
    ) +
    geom_hline(yintercept = -log10(pval_cutoff), linetype = "dashed", color = "black") +
    geom_vline(xintercept = c(-fc_cutoff, fc_cutoff), linetype = "dashed", color = "black") +
    scale_color_manual(values = c("Up" = "red", "Down" = "blue", "NS" = "grey")) +
    labs(
      title = paste(group1, "vs", group2),
      x = "Log2 Distance",
      y = "-Log10 Adjusted p-value",
      color = "Regulation"
    ) +
    theme_minimal(base_size = 14)
  
  
  ggsave(file.path(subdir_path, paste0("Volcano_", comp_name, ".svg")), plot = p1,width = 10, height = 8)
  
  # DMA CORE ZOOMED PLOT
  p1_zoom <- ggplot(DMA_results_df, aes(x = Log2_Distance, y = neglog10_pval)) +
    geom_point(aes(color = Significance), size = 2) +
    geom_text_repel(
      data = subset(DMA_results_df, Significance != "NS"),
      aes(label = feature),
      size = 3,
      max.overlaps = 50,
      box.padding = 0.3,
      segment.color = "grey50"
    ) +
    geom_hline(yintercept = -log10(pval_cutoff), linetype = "dashed", color = "black") +
    geom_vline(xintercept = c(-fc_cutoff, fc_cutoff), linetype = "dashed", color = "black") +
    scale_color_manual(values = c("Up" = "red", "Down" = "blue", "NS" = "grey")) +
    labs(
      title = paste(group1, "vs", group2),
      x = "Log2 Distance",
      y = "-Log10 Adjusted p-value",
      color = "Regulation"
    ) +
    theme_minimal(base_size = 14)
  
  ggsave(file.path(subdir_path, paste0("Volcano_", comp_name, "_ZOOM.svg")),
         plot = p1_zoom,  width = 28, height = 18, limitsize = FALSE)
  
  
  
  ###### note core
}else{
  VolcanoPlot <- EnhancedVolcano(
    DMA_results_df,
    lab = DMA_results_df$feature,
    x = 'log2FC',
    y = 'p.adjusted',
    title = paste0(gsub("_", " ", comp_name)),
    subtitle = 'Differential Metabolite Analysis (t-test)',
    xlab = bquote(~Log[2]~Fold~Change),
    ylab = bquote(~-Log[10]~Adjusted~p~value),
    pCutoff = pval_cutoff,
    FCcutoff = fc_cutoff,
    pointSize = 2.5,
    labSize = 3,
    colAlpha = 1,
    legendPosition = 'right',
    drawConnectors = T,
    ylim = c(0, max_y <- ceiling(max(-log10(DMA_results_df$p.adjusted), na.rm = TRUE) + 0.5))
  )
  
  
  ggsave(paste0(subdir_path, "/Volcano_",comp_name,".svg"), plot = VolcanoPlot, width = 10, height = 8)
  
  
  
  ##ZOOMED volcano
  VolcanoPlot <- EnhancedVolcano(
    DMA_results_df,
    lab = DMA_results_df$feature,
    x = 'log2FC',
    y = 'p.adjusted',
    title = paste0(gsub("_", " ", comp_name)),
    subtitle = 'Differential Metabolite Analysis (t-test)',
    xlab = bquote(~Log[2]~Fold~Change),
    ylab = bquote(~-Log[10]~Adjusted~p~value),
    pCutoff = pval_cutoff,
    FCcutoff = fc_cutoff,
    pointSize = 2.1,
    labSize = 5,
    colAlpha = 1,
    legendPosition = 'right',
    arrowheads = FALSE,
    drawConnectors = T,
    ylim = c(0, max_y <- ceiling(max(-log10(DMA_results_df$p.adjusted), na.rm = TRUE) + 0.5))
  )
  # Save plot
  ggsave(paste0(subdir_path, "/Volcano_",comp_name,"_ZOOM.svg"), plot = VolcanoPlot, width = 50, height = 32,limitsize = FALSE)
  
}


```

#Pathview
```{r}
if(CoRe==FALSE){
  Pathview_dir <- file.path(output.dir, "6. Pathview")
if (!dir.exists(Pathview_dir)) dir.create(Pathview_dir, recursive = TRUE)

subdir_path <- file.path(Pathview_dir, comp_name)
# 2. Create the subdirectory if it doesn't exist
if (!dir.exists(subdir_path)) {
  dir.create(subdir_path, recursive = TRUE)
}
# 3. Set as working directory
setwd(subdir_path)

rownames(universe) <- universe$Input.name
tmp <- merge(DMA_results_df,    universe%>% select(KEGG_ID),by=0)
fc_vector <- setNames(tmp$log2FC, tmp$KEGG_ID)
# Clean up NA values in your fc_vector
fc_vector <- fc_vector[!is.na(fc_vector)]
# List of KEGG pathway IDs
kegg_paths <- c(
  "hsa00010", "hsa00030", "hsa00020", "hsa00190",
  "hsa00250", "hsa00330", "hsa00260", "hsa00280",
  "hsa00230", "hsa00240", "hsa00480", "hsa00760", "hsa00520"
)

path_names <- c(
  hsa00010 = "Glycolysis",
  hsa00030 = "Pentose_Phosphate",
  hsa00020 = "TCA",
  hsa00190 = "OxPhos",
  hsa00250 = "Ala_Asp_Glu",
  hsa00330 = "Arg_Pro",
  hsa00260 = "Gly_Ser_Thr",
  hsa00280 = "Val_Leu_Ile",
  hsa00230 = "Purine",
  hsa00240 = "Pyrimidine",
  hsa00480 = "Glutathione",
  hsa00760 = "Nicotinate",
  hsa00520 = "Amino_Sugar"
)


for (path_id in names(path_names)) {
  pathview(
    cpd.data = fc_vector,
    pathway.id = path_id,
    species = "hsa",
    kegg.native = TRUE,
    out.suffix = path_names[[path_id]],
    cpd.idtype = "kegg"
  )
}

## remove unsessesary files
files <- list.files()
# Pattern: ends with number(s), dot, and exactly 3 lowercase letters
pattern <- "\\d+\\.[a-z]{3}$"
# Find matching files
files_to_remove <- files[grepl(pattern, files)]
# Remove them
file.remove(files_to_remove)

}
```

# Ora
```{r}
Ora_dir <- file.path(output.dir, "5. ORA")
if (!dir.exists(Ora_dir)) dir.create(Ora_dir, recursive = TRUE)

rm(mSet)
subdir_path <- file.path(Ora_dir, comp_name)
# 2. Create the subdirectory if it doesn't exist
if (!dir.exists(subdir_path)) {
  dir.create(subdir_path, recursive = TRUE)
}
# 3. Set as working directory
setwd(subdir_path)

# Make a list of metabs
if(CoRe==T){
  ora_list <- DMA_results_df %>% filter(abs(Log2_Distance)>0.5 & p.adjusted<pval_cutoff) %>% select(feature)  %>% unlist() %>% as.vector()
}else{
  ora_list <- DMA_results_df %>% filter(abs(log2FC)>0.5 & p.adjusted<pval_cutoff) %>% select(feature)  %>% unlist() %>% as.vector()
}
# Remove any asterisks
ora_list <- gsub("\\*", "", ora_list)
ora_list <- gsub("†", "", ora_list)

ora_list <- gsub(  "CAR 3:0 Propionylcarnitine", "Propionylcarnitine", ora_list)
ora_list <- gsub( "CAR 6:0 Hexanoylcarnitine" , "Hexanoylcarnitine", ora_list)

ora_list <- ora_list %>% na.omit()

#ora_list <- gsub("-", " ", ora_list)
checkdf <- data.frame(ora_list)

name <- comp_name

# Create vector consisting of compounds for enrichment analysis 
tmp.vec <- ora_list

# Create mSetObj
mSet<-InitDataObjects("conc", "msetora", FALSE, default.dpi = 300)

#Set up mSetObj with the list of compounds
mSet<-Setup.MapData(mSet, tmp.vec);

# Cross reference list of compounds against libraries (hmdb, pubchem, chebi, kegg, metlin)
mSet<-CrossReferencing(mSet, "name");

# Create the mapping results table
mSet<-CreateMappingResultTable(mSet)

Check <- read.csv(file.path(subdir_path, "name_map.csv"))
if(length(Check$Match[!is.na(Check$Match)])>5){
  # Set the metabolite filter
  mSet<-SetMetabolomeFilter(mSet, F);
  
  # # Select metabolite set library, refer to 
  # mSet<-SetCurrentMsetLib(mSet, "smpdb_pathway", 0); #kegg_pathway or smpdb_pathway
  # # Calculate hypergeometric score, results table generated in your working directory
  # mSet<-CalculateHyperScore(mSet)
  # or this
  # Initialize mSetObj for offline mode
  mSet <- InitDataObjects("conc", "msetora", FALSE, default.dpi = 300)  # Set TRUE for offline mode
  
  # Set pathway library location
  mSet <- SetCurrentMsetLib(mSet, "smpdb_pathway", 0)  # Replace "kegg_pathway" as needed
  
  # Load your pathway data locally
  # Ensure pathway files are downloaded and accessible in the working directory
  mSet <- CalculateHyperScore(mSet)
  
  ora_mat <-as.data.frame( mSet$analSet$ora.mat)
  # Add enrichment ratio column
  ora_mat$EnrichmentRatio <- ora_mat$hits / ora_mat$expected
  
  mSet$analSet$ora.mat <- ora_mat
  
  # Order by enrichment ratio (descending)
  mSet$analSet$ora.mat <- mSet$analSet$ora.mat[order(mSet$analSet$ora.mat$EnrichmentRatio, decreasing = TRUE), ]
  
  
  # Plot the ORA, bar-graph
  mSet<-PlotORA(mSet, paste0("Plot_ORA_",comp_name,"_smpdb_pathway_"), "bar", "pdf", 72, width=NA)
  
  OraRes <- mSet$analSet$ora.mat %>% as.data.frame()
  # OraRes <- rownames_to_column(OraRes, "Pathway")
  # writexl::write_xlsx(OraRes, paste0(name,"_OraRes.xlsx"))
  rm(mSet)
  
  # find metabs to select
  dt_saved <- qs::qread("smpdb_pathway.qs")
  writexl::write_xlsx(dt_saved, paste0("Path-Metab_assignment",comp_name,".xlsx"))
  
  setwd(ProjectFolder)
}
```



##### TST METAPROVIZ
```{r}
library(MetaProViz)
# MappingInfo <- MetaProViz::ToyData(Data="Cells_MetaData")
DMA_Annova <-  MetaProViz::dma(data=processed_data,
                               metadata_sample=SSF,
```
